project_name: Fostering innovative and sustainable bio-waste management and circular bio-economy in the Mediterranean area
project_summary: CirBioWaste helps Mediterranean regions improve bio-waste management by promoting sustainable, circular practices. Despite EU rules, only 16% of food waste is properly collected and recycled, with many areas still relying on landfills. The project supports local governments and universities in six countries by adapting proven solutions, improving collection systems, boosting composting, and encouraging use of organic fertilizers. It combines local and international efforts, offering training, tools, and shared strategies to reduce environmental impact and support a circular bioeconomy across the EU MED region+B20.

project_name: Port-City Regions To Wave Med Cruise Destinations As Smart, Sustainable And Striking Eco-Tourism Systems
partner_name: Fondazione Istituto sui Trasporti e la Logistica
topic_keywords: education | workshop | pilot action | pilot actions | platform | online platform
project_summary:
Cruise tourists are increasingly seeking tailor-made inland tours. They want to discover more authentic places in a sustainable way, in tours that suit their curiosity as well as their physical abilities.
The eco-tourism platform PortCREW will be tested in 5 Mediterranean cruise ports located in Spain, France, Italy, Slovenia, and Greece. What is it about? Providing cruise managers, port or tourism authorities with new inland tourism packages. How will it help tourism management? By avoiding overtourism in the same seaside areas, valorising new natural and cultural assets and increasing the social acceptance of the cruise sector in MED cities.

project_name: Developing Inclusive Education in the Mediterranean
programme: 2021 - 2027 Interreg VI-B NEXT Mediterranean Sea Basin (NEXT MED)
topic_keywords: training | trainings | learning | education | course | skills | capacity building | capacity development | capacity | knowledge transfer | toolkit | dissemination | communication | replication | scalability | beneficiaries | youth | women | communities
project_summary:
PROJECT TYPE: 1 - Thematic projects
~
MAIN INTERVENTION FIELD:

151. Support for adult education (excluding infrastructure)
~
SUMMARY: The Mediterranean is united by some major challenges related to the development of its different areas and communities, especially regarding the gap between cultural heritage developmental potential and the social and economic precariousness prevailing around important cultural sites.
Education, continuous training and skills development represent a key tool both for strengthening the potential of territories and for promoting inclusion, empowerment and social cohesion.
Furthermore, Cultural Heritage (CH) is one of the most sound, common and recognizable Mediterranean traits as well as an acknowledged driver for its development.
Accordingly, its main objective will be reinforcing and innovating the consortium's different learning/teaching systems to make them more integrated and compatible. Besides, the focus on Cultural Heritage will allow DIEM to exploit the acknowledged multiplier effect of CH-oriented activities in terms of know-how exchanging, empowerment of people and, eventually, economic growth.
Hence, DIEM is expected to contribute to social inclusion and to skills development for the unintegrated groups (youth, women, rural inhabitants and NEETs). Capitalizing on the experience of its partners, DIEM will move along 3 concurrent lines of development: deliver a cascade model of teaching/training programs for 3 groups (A-Leaders, B-Future Managers, C-Local communities); setup a toolkit for teaching/learning capable to combine knowledge, emerging technologies (i.e. AI) and the lessons learned from the pandemic; assume DIEM as an occasion for the first beneficiaries and for broader communities, the assets and territories belonging to the 6 countries of DIEM consortium (IT, EGY, JOR, TUN, ES,PS).
The project entails 3 technical WPs: Standardization (Partners design systems for international cooperation), Training around pilot areas (TOT courses and inclusion-oriented trainings), Scalability (replication on youth and extension to broader communities).
~
KEEP KEYWORDS: Cultural heritage and arts, Education and training, Social inclusion and equal opportunities
specific_objective: RSO4.2. Improving equal access to inclusive and quality services in education, training and lifelong learning through developing accessible infrastructure, including by fostering resilience for distance and on-line education and training

Capitalizing on the experience of its partners, DIEM will move along 3 concurrent lines of development: deliver a cascade model of teaching/training programs for 3 groups (A-Leaders, B-Future Managers, C-Local communities); setup a toolkit for teaching/learning capable to combine knowledge, emerging technologies (i.e. AI) and the lessons learned from the pandemic; assume DIEM as an occasion for the first beneficiaries and for broader communities, the assets and territories belonging to the 6 countries of DIEM consortium (IT, EGY, JOR, TUN, ES,PS).
The project entails 3 technical WPs: Standardization (Partners design systems for international cooperation), Training around pilot areas (TOT courses and inclusion-oriented trainings), Scalability (replication on youth and extension to broader communities).
~

project_summary:
PROJECT TYPE: 2 - Youth-oriented projects
~
MAIN INTERVENTION FIELD:
~
SUMMARY: The educational landscape lacks comprehensive coverage of the socio-political, economic & technical dimensions of the ecosystem, hindering the root causes & ramifications of the eco-crisis. Our project aims to develop a holistic environmental training program for higher education students & an advocacy initiative through network building. We strive to transform individuals' perceptions of social & environmental ecosystems & confront nature preservation challenges through interdisciplinary training driven by the collaborative efforts of academics, researchers, & experts from diverse disciplines.
The environmental crisis is complex & part of a global polycrisis. Despite local variations, its systematic nature shares common traits. National actors often focus on local symptoms without addressing underlying causes. The systematic structure of the environmental crisis calls for collective solutions based on contemporary approaches & evidence-based knowledge.
Within this scope our goal is to
-Strengthen interdisciplinary collaboration to develop & promote consensus-based approaches & solutions for green transition
-Foster knowledge sharing through training programs highlighting the interconnected nature of environmental & social ecosystems
-Enhance blue & green upskilling among higher education students
-Build capacity for long-term network & advocacy

By enhancing blue & green upskilling, we anticipate a significant shift in how young adults perceive & address environmental challenges. The training programs will nurture solution-oriented mindsets among university students entering the workforce. By fostering interdisciplinary collaboration & empowering young adults with skills, we envision a generation equipped to tackle environmental issues in their everyday lives. Beneficiaries extend beyond higher education students to society as a whole. In the long term, we aim to create a ripple effect impacting sectors, & actors, with broader adult education through our network.
~

project_summary:
PROJECT TYPE: 3 - Governance Projects
~
MAIN INTERVENTION FIELD:

173. Enhancing institutional capacity of public authorities and stakeholders to implement territorial cooperation projects and initiatives in a cross-border, transnational, maritime and inter-regional context
~
SUMMARY: The lack of participatory planning for social inclusion programs on the part of local authorities has led to a misguided conception in identifying real needs and inclusive understanding of the pressing social challenges across the Mediterranean. This has hindered the development of society driven solutions that effectively respond to these challenges. TRANSFORMED will foster cross-sector synergies through a transnational cooperation to jointly develop solutions to facilitate the establishment of efficient governance models for improving social inclusion at local level.
The project proposal comprises analysing, complementing and disseminating the transnational knowledge demonstrated in implementing 4 regionally-acclaimed governance models that have adopted bottom-up and participatory approaches to deliver inclusive community-serving programs; the objective being to exploit and upscale this knowledge.
The outputs are complementary, using a building blocks approach. They are:
-Transnational Handbook, that is a fruit of 5 transnational Community-engagement symposia, Online data analysis clusters and a practitioners’ lab;
-Enhanced institutional capacities of 5 local authorities and 5 social inclusion civic actors, thanks to the project’s call for inclusive Local Planning concept notes, the Peer-to-peer knowledge capsules and the procedure demonstration pilots;
- 5 Blueprints with jointly developed social inclusion community-serving programs operated by tandems between local authorities and civil society actors responding to inclusion challenges identified on the national level. Inclusive Local Planning Blueprints resulting from Peer-to-peer mentoring, enhanced by national round table debates and will be introduced in a regional knowledge sharing exhibition.
Final beneficiaries are local authorities and civic actors from partner countries benefiting from best practices of participatory governance, and local communities benefiting from the improved local governance.
~

project_summary:
PROJECT TYPE: 1 - Thematic projects
~
MAIN INTERVENTION FIELD: 059. Adaptation to climate change measures and prevention and management of climate related risks: fires (including awareness raising, civil protection and disaster management systems, infrastructures and ecosystem-based approaches)
~
SUMMARY: The MedFireWise addresses the escalating threat of wildfires in the Med forests by promoting prescribed burning & silvopasture as Nature-based Solutions (NbS) for wildfire risk management (WRFM). Through transnational cooperation, it aims to mitigate wildfire risk in ecosystems, leveraging ecological processes to enhance resilience & reduce fire intensity & severity. The development of innovative tools & frameworks will enhance situational awareness for evaluating sustainable interventions in strategic forest management areas, thereby refining risk management systems. The implementation in 7 regions will demonstrate the effectiveness of the NbS, benefiting local communities, economies & ecosystems. It will provide tools for more effective WRFM, increase stakeholder capacity, raise public awareness & transfer its outcomes to regions among & beyond the project areas, contributing to more resilient Med forests to confront the climate change impacts.
Main Outputs (O):
WP3: O3.1 Report on needs, regulatory framework & existing NbS of partner countries in WFRM, O3.2 Framework of indicators for the implementation of NbS & O3.3 Seasonal WRFM tool
WP4: O4.1 Cross-Campaigns design, O4.2 Forestry works to facilitate the execution of prescribed burns & silvopasture & O4.3 Cross-Campaigns implementation
WP5: O5.1 Increased risk awareness of local community in wildfire-prone areas & O5.2 Strategy for transferring solutions to regions beyond partnership
MedFireWise will produce tools & facilitate resources that will leave a significant impact on the beneficiaries: enhanced protection against wildfires to local communities in high-risk areas; better tools for environmental & risk managers to effectively manage wildfire risks while promoting ecosystem restoration & functionality; safe forest infrastructure for firefighting services; new grazing opportunities in strategic areas for shepherds; evidence-based methods for policy-makers to promote wildfire-related policies.
~

partner_name: Fundación Cámara de Comercio, Industria, Servicios y Navegación de Sevilla

project_summary:
PROJECT TYPE: 2 - Youth-oriented projects
~
MAIN INTERVENTION FIELD: 152. Measures to promote equal opportunities and active participation in society
~
SUMMARY: YOUTHMED addresses critical challenges in the MED region identified in reports by UfM & the United Nations, such as limited access to education & economic opportunities, gender-based discrimination, & social exclusion among youth & migrants. By fostering critical thinking, enhancing skills, & promoting social inclusion, YOUTHMED aims to empower marginalized groups & build cohesive communities through a collaborative approach with partners from Lebanon, Greece, Spain, & Egypt.
The project tackles three main challenges highlighted in the UfM Regional Strategy for Youth & the UN's Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs): limited training & economic opportunities for marginalized youth, gender-based discrimination, & a lack of cross-border collaboration among youth organizations. YOUTHMED will equip youth with skills in digital marketing, social media, & critical thinking while promoting gender equality & social inclusion. It will also strengthen cross-border cooperation among youth organizations.
Key outputs include a cross-border cooperation agreement between 25 organizations, development of an e-media exchange platform, a comprehensive training curriculum, & capacity building for 480 marginalized youth & 30 members of youth organizations. Additionally, 300 participants will engage in awareness campaigns, & 75 vulnerable individuals will receive counseling & vocational training.
The main beneficiaries are marginalized youth, vulnerable groups including women, refugees, & migrants, & local communities. By integrating environmental sustainability into its design, YOUTHMED will promote digital collaboration, eco-friendly event practices, & raise awareness about environmental protection.
YOUTHMED aims to create lasting positive impacts for marginalized youth & migrants in the MED region. it is designed to influence policy changes, build institutional capacity, & establish sustainable partnerships that will continue to support youth empowerment beyond the project's duration.
~

project_summary:
PROJECT TYPE: 1 - Thematic projects
~
MAIN INTERVENTION FIELD: 060. Adaptation to climate change measures and prevention and management of climate related risks: others, e.g. storms and drought (including awareness raising, civil protection and disaster management systems, infrastructures and ecosystem based approaches)
~
SUMMARY: The MedPROACT project aims to enhance the adaptive capacity of Mediterranean coastal communities to climate change by focusing on ecosystem resilience and sustainable management of marine resources. This initiative involves a transnational consortium of environmental NGOs, universities, and research institutions from Italy, Turkey, Jordan, and Tunisia. The activities are organised into three technical work packages that address, through demonstrated solutions and tested pilot actions, various aspects of climate change adaptation such as the management of invasive species, restoration of specific marine ecosystems, and development of local climate adaptation tools.
Expected outcomes:
- the adaptive capacity of coastal communities connected to fishery and seafood consumption is increased by promoting knowledge and strategies that address the invasive species proliferation, reducing pressure on traditional stocks and increasing scientific literacy;
- improved ecosystem resilience of seagrass meadows and coral reefs through restoration and sustainable management practices taught to local communities, including the decision makers of marine protected areas, in order to allow the maintenance of their crucial role in carbon sequestration, coastal protection, and habitats supporting marine life;
- enhanced community resilience: by increasing awareness of the local population and developing effective adaptation policies through participative processes, the project aims to equip the local communities and authorities with effective Adaptation Plans and Guidelines, governance tools that will safeguard people, activities, tourists and livelihoods, especially connected to marine and coastal resources.
In summary, MedPROACT leverages a comprehensive, multilevel approach to build resilience against climate change in Mediterranean coastal communities and benefit local actors such as fishers, tourism operators, scientific community, governmental bodies, youths.
~

project_summary:
PROJECT TYPE: 1 - Thematic projects
~
MAIN INTERVENTION FIELD:

160. Measures to improve the accessibility, effectiveness and resilience of healthcare systems (excluding infrastructure)
~
SUMMARY: SALAM project, led by Polyclinic of Bari, brings together technological universities from Italy (POLIBA), Spain (University of Barcelona), Jordan (JUST), and Lebanon (University of Balamand), and the two Caritas from Lebanon and Jordan, intertwining scientific-technological and humanistic-social demands. It addresses the crisis of public health systems in the countries involved in the project, expressed differently but with common aspects such as shortage of healthcare providers and resources,impacting on equity of access to health.
Human complexity requires a more holistic approach to healthcare, including highly specialised, competent, flexible, resilient, and cooperative healthcare professionalism.
Within Open Innovation methodological framework, which underlies synergies between different, complementary disciplines, SALAM will develop a comprehensive solution that will bring mutual benefits to involved countries, envisaging:
Two hospital hubs in Lebanon and Jordan, integrated with telecardiology service, Helis Infarct.NET platform and related equipment, in use at the Polyclinic of Bari, which will be scaled up and uptaken with the support of academic partners. The hubs will include Medical Mobile Units and will contribute to making right to health concrete.
Innovative sanitaire protocols for medical staff training and for raising awareness among caregivers and local populations, to ensure the functioning of the HUBs, promote knowledge exchange and empower communities on new medical practices.
Transnational Research Center among partner universities on both sides of Mediterranean, which, moving from health needs of territories, will work together to develop accessible medical bio-devices, benefiting everyone - especially those affected by CVDs - so that "no one is left behind" and the value of inclusion can find substance across different nations, in a relationship of mutual enrichment where peace will be direct consequence of well-being for all.
~

project_name: Strategic Integration of Operational and Legal Frameworks for Enhanced Resilience and Management of Mediterranean Wildfires
programme: 2021 - 2027 Interreg VI-B NEXT Mediterranean Sea Basin (NEXT MED)
topic_keywords: training | workshop | training materials | capacity | knowledge transfer | technology transfer | toolkit | awareness | awareness raising | communication | transferability | sme | platform | volunteers | communities
project_summary:
PROJECT TYPE: 1 - Thematic projects
~
MAIN INTERVENTION FIELD: 059. Adaptation to climate change measures and prevention and management of climate related risks: fires (including awareness raising, civil protection and disaster management systems, infrastructures and ecosystem-based approaches)
~
SUMMARY: Wildfires are worsening in the Mediterranean due to longer fire seasons, and mega-fires. A key challenge is ensuring wildfire management uses science-based innovations. SIMMER invites scientists, practitioners, and decision-makers to collaborate, share best practices to improve management. This includes understanding wildfire behavior, trends, and climate change impacts to better manage landscapes, ecosystems, and response effectiveness.
SIMMER uniquely analyzes the interplay between scientific, legal, and operationalization context to develop the cornerstone Integrated Science-Policy Framework for sustainable wildfire management. It bridges the gap between advanced technological tools and their practical application, enhancing stakeholders' management capacity across the entire lifecycle—from preparedness to response, and recovery.
SIMMER holistic approach starts by collecting stakeholder needs, mapping existing wildfire management assets, developing maturity indicators, and identifying gaps. Next, SIMMER creates the integrated science-policy framework to address these gaps, which is tested through pilot activities. In the final phase, SIMMER produces a Knowledge Hub and Networking Platform, drafting policy recommendations, and training materials based on lessons learned.
SIMMER pilot activities in six countries involve civil protection, nature protection organizations, local authorities, and volunteers. Demonstrable tools include ecosystem-based methods like prescribed burning and grazing, AI, remote sensing, for risk assessment and early warning, drones for burn severity mapping, and community-based post-fire assessment.
SIMMER offers stakeholders mature scientific solutions, legal frameworks and operationalization protocols, benefiting decision-makers, firefighters, community leaders, and researchers. Beyond providing tools, SIMMER fosters collaboration and knowledge sharing, empowering communities to proactively protect themselves and their environment.
~
KEEP KEYWORDS: Climate change and biodiversity, Innovation capacity and awareness-raising, Knowledge and technology transfer

The TO CARE MED project will extend the TCCL tool to other coastal, island, rural and protected natural tourist destinations, focusing on two aspects. project_name: Strategic Harmonization and Resource Enhancement in the MEDiterranean: Advancing Water Management and Innovation through Non-Conventional Resources.
partner_name: Εθνικό Κέντρο Έρευνας Και Τεχνολογικής Ανάπτυξης (Πτολεμαΐδα)
topic_keywords: education | capacity building | capacity | presentation | uptake | reuse | scalable | implementation plan | sme | communities
project_summary:
SHARE.MedWATER delivers scalable solutions to reuse rainwater, greywater, and treated wastewater in rural and island regions across seven Mediterranean countries.

It sets up community-level pilots where rainwater is harvested to irrigate crops, greywater from homes is reused for toilet flushing, and treated wastewater supports green spaces. At district level, two large-scale demos coordinate water reuse across agriculture, tourism, and urban services.

Inspired by energy-sharing models, SHARE.MedWATER builds a framework for managing and distributing non-conventional water sources collectively. A decision-support tool helps authorities weigh costs, environmental impact, and user needs. Through transnational cooperation, it aims to reduce dependence on freshwater, strengthen climate resilience, and support EU and regional water strategies.
specific_objective: RSO1.1. Developing and enhancing research and innovation capacities and the uptake of advanced technologies

project_name: Strategic Harmonization and Resource Enhancement in the MEDiterranean: Advancing Water Management and Innovation through Non-Conventional Resources.
partner_name: ENEA Agenzia nazionale per le nuove tecnologie, l'energia e lo sviluppo economico sostenibile
topic_keywords: capacity building | capacity | presentation | uptake | reuse | scalable | implementation plan | sme | communities
project_summary:
SHARE.MedWATER delivers scalable solutions to reuse rainwater, greywater, and treated wastewater in rural and island regions across seven Mediterranean countries.

It sets up community-level pilots where rainwater is harvested to irrigate crops, greywater from homes is reused for toilet flushing, and treated wastewater supports green spaces. At district level, two large-scale demos coordinate water reuse across agriculture, tourism, and urban services.

Inspired by energy-sharing models, SHARE.MedWATER builds a framework for managing and distributing non-conventional water sources collectively. A decision-support tool helps authorities weigh costs, environmental impact, and user needs. Through transnational cooperation, it aims to reduce dependence on freshwater, strengthen climate resilience, and support EU and regional water strategies.

project_name: Creative craft for industrial symbiosis in blue-economy sectors - RECONNECT
topic_keywords: methodology | toolkit | guidelines | uptake | sme | smes | business support | communities
project_summary: RECONNECT aims to boost the circular economy in the Mediterranean by reusing waste from fisheries and aquaculture to create valuable bio-based products. It promotes collaboration between industries, craft-based businesses, and communities using innovative technology and sustainable business models. The project highlights the role of crafts in driving creative and social approaches to circularity. It supports cooperation between sectors that rarely interact, benefiting both large industries and small firms. RECONNECT will test its tools with 130 SMEs and create networks and resources to ensure long-term impact in sustainable blue economy practices.

project_name: Resilience ENhancing to adapt FORests to Climate change Effects and to prevent biotic and abiotic hazard
partner_name: ENTE DI GESTIONE DEI SACRI MONTI
project_summary:
In Mediterranean regions hit by wildfires and windstorms, RENFORCE shifts forest management from reaction to prevention. In other words, the project develops practical tools to cut risks before disaster strikes.
It sets up participatory prevention plans in pilot zones, combining local knowledge with forestry science. On the ground, this means mapping high-risk areas, managing vegetation to act as natural firebreaks, and applying storm-resistant planting strategies.
RENFORCE also builds common protocols across borders to anticipate damage. It delivers decision-making tools for public authorities, training programmes for landowners, and a shared strategy to embed risk reduction into forest planning.

project_name: Promoting a Circular Economy with Low Plastic Zones on Mediterranean Islands
topic_keywords: learning | methodology | guidelines | replication | business support | platform
project_summary:
LooP Zone takes on one of the Mediterranean’s most visible challenges: the plastic waste crisis on tourist islands. With over 200 million visitors each year, and single-use plastics (SUPs) products, making up more than 60% of marine litter, island territories like Formentera, Salina, Skiathos, Vis, and Gozo face growing pressure—especially as most local waste management systems can’t keep up.

LooP Zone creates “low plastic zones” where municipalities, tourism businesses, and local innovators act together to prevent and phase out SUPs. Hotels, beach bars and restaurants receive tools to assess their plastic footprint and adopt sustainable and reusable alternatives; new value chains for SUP-free products are supported through innovation labs; and local policies are strengthened to align with national bans.

The model, built on a 3-pillar approach—policy, business support, and innovation—is tested locally, transferred and accessible via a digital platform, and scaled through a regional island network. The goal: transform the tourism sector from a take-make-waste model to a circular economy that works for both people and the sea.
specific_objective: RSO2.6. Promoting the transition to a circular and resource efficient economy

project_name: Development of Sustainable Astrotourism in Euro-MED Regions
project_summary:
Astrotourism is a new type of tourism in nature areas that focuses on the observation of celestial objects. Specifically, it consists of hiking and astronomy tours, observation of the nocturnal fauna, off-grid hotels, tourist services offered by astronomical observatories...

The MedSkyTour project has two main objectives: to develop this type of tourism and to implement actions aimed at reducing light pollution. In this way, the eight regions involved are not only creating new growth opportunities for sustainable tourism but are also generating real benefits for nocturnal biodiversity.
specific_objective: RSO2.7. Enhancing protection and preservation of nature, biodiversity and green infrastructure, including in urban areas, and reducing all forms of pollution

project_name: Inclusive Coastal Resilience in the Euro-Med
topic_keywords: course | workshop | methodology | methodologies | dissemination | communication | sme
project_summary:
INCORE-MED brings citizens back into the heart of coastal planning. In Italy, Spain, Portugal, and Croatia, the project tests two advanced participation tools to improve coastal and marine policies: SWAP and Democracy Labs.
The SWAP tool (Scenario Workshops + Adaptation Pathways) helps local actors co-design realistic climate adaptation plans by blending community visions with expert climate projections, using scenario mapping and multicriteria analysis. As for Democracy Labs, it creates discussion spaces where citizens and authorities co-develop new rules and formats for public engagement in marine planning.
These tools are deployed to: 1) create and update participatory cartographies and climate risk perception maps; 2) integrate nature-based solutions in local action plans; 3) disseminate the methodology to new territories through methodological insights and online courses.

project_name: Towards a Mediterranean model of Regenerative Tourism as Climate Change adaptation and risk prevention
topic_keywords: training | education | academy | capacity building | capacity | sme | communities
project_summary:
More than 70% of European citizens say they are willing to change their travel habits to a more sustainable model. Regenera4MED wants to go further!
Its concept of Regenerative Tourism goes beyond sustainability, it actively seeks to restore and revitalize natural ecosystems, strengthen local cultures, and empower communities. It promotes climate change adaptation and resilience while fostering meaningful connections that generate long-term, shared value for both people and the planet.
Regenera4MED will be tested in 6 high-profile tourist destinations: Catalonia (Spain), Corsica (France), Liguria and Abruzzo (Italy), Split-Dalmatia (Croatia) and Crete (Greece). Its long-term goals? To reposition the Mediterranean as a global benchmark for Regenerative tourism and to promote the region as a destination for the tourism of the future.

project_name: Monitoring cumulative Impact to guide mitigation and RestorAtion in the MediterrAnean Region
partner_name: Agencia Estatal Consejo Superior de Investigaciones Científicas - Instituto Español de Oceanografia
topic_keywords: training | training materials | capacity | methodology | toolkit | toolkits | stakeholder engagement | presenting | sme
project_summary: Humans are placing increasing strain on ecosystems exposing them to multiple, concurrent stressors. The Mediterranean region is a biodiversity hotspot hosting almost half of all the plant species, about a third of all the vertebrate species on our planet and an exceptional number of species found nowhere else in the world. This biodiversity, however, is heavily exposed to stressors like legacy and emerging contaminants, marine litter including microplastics, noise, habitat destruction from ever-increasing human activities, exacerbated by climate change, resulting in long-term impacts on ecosystem stability. These stressors are often tackled in isolation. The urgency to address these threats and the cumulative impact of multiple stressors requires cohesive, transnational efforts that combine fragmented knowledge to develop a new, integrated approach. In response to these challenges, the MIRAMAR project has identified three ecologically significant ecosystems in the Euro-Mediterranean region: seagrass meadows, wetlands, and habitats of endangered species. Seagrass meadows serve as crucial breeding, feeding, and sheltering grounds for numerous species, are vital for oxygen production and as blue carbon sinks. Wetlands are biodiverse transition ecosystems containing river deltas, brackish waters, salt lakes, and marshes, supporting a variety of flora and fauna, that play a critical role in maintaining ecological balance. Healthy endangered species habitats, including those of seabirds, cetaceans and wetland birds, are vital for the survival of these vulnerable populations. MIRAMAR’s 8 partners represent 6 countries across the Euro-Med region (Albania, Croatia, France, Greece, Italy, and Spain), and come from academia (UNISI), research institutes (CSIC, HCMR, SZN, RBI), NGO networks (MIO-ECSDE), as well as regional (OEC) and national authorities (MTE).Thanks to this transnational collaboration, MIRAMAR aims to develop an innovative harmonized methodology to monitor cumulative impact of selected stressors in 9 pilot areas in the three identified habitats. Drawing support and upscaling previous project results (INTERREG, ADRION, LIFE, H2020) MIRAMAR will design, test, and validate a new holistic monitoring method to identify suitable nature-based mitigation and restoration solutions. By aligning with national monitoring strategies and the management plans of parks and protected areas, MIRAMAR will address the common challenge of preventing, mitigating and restoring environmental degradation of these ecosystems. MIRAMAR is committed to aligning with key environmental initiatives and programs such as, Zero Pollution Action Plan of the European Green Deal, EU Biodiversity Strategy for 2030, EU Marine Strategy Framework Directive, Barcelona Convention's Integrated Monitoring and Assessment Programme, Union for the Mediterranean, UN Environment Programme/Mediterranean Action Plan, Ramsar Convention on Wetlands, among others. These frameworks emphasize the interconnected nature of climate change, biodiversity loss, land and sea degradation, stressing the importance of addressing these challenges in a coherent, synergetic, and holistic manner based on best available scientific knowledge.By engaging with these international agreements and frameworks, MIRAMAR will contribute to global efforts to tackle environmental issues and promote sustainable development practices. MIRAMAR’s collaborative approach and focus on stakeholder engagement, benefitting from Living Labs, underscores our commitment to harnessing collective expertise and fostering innovative solutions to address the complex challenges facing the Euro-Mediterranean region. Fourteen associate partners, representing key stakeholders in each targeted pilot area, will be proactively engaged in the project. They will see their scientific knowledge and capacity to implement environmental management practices and policies increase; more importantly, they will benefit from the project outcomes through the monitoring protocols and operational roadmap. This process of engagement will take concrete form with the signing of Declaration of Intent to adopt MIRAMARs harmonised monitoring protocol to assess cumulative impacts, as a key commitment to identify the "best" mitigation and restoration solutions. Through partnerships with diverse stakeholders, alignment with international initiatives, and a focus on science-based solutions, MIRAMAR is positioned to make a meaningful impact to protect nature, biodiversity, and green infrastructure for the benefit of current and future generations. With a comprehensive and inclusive approach, MIRAMAR aims to lead the way in addressing complex environmental challenges and charting a resilient, biodiverse, and sustainable future for all.

project_summary: The INSPIRE project wants to transform agrobiodiversity conservation into touristic opportunities. Agrobiodiversity is the term used to describe the way natural ecosystems work in areas of farming activities.How can this be achieved? First, by getting the 8 partners working on designing a sustainable tourism model based on agrobiodiversity. It will then be tested in five regions, where public and private tourism groups, farmers, and local communities will work together to apply it. In the end, all these initiatives would be transferable to a wider sample of territories in the future!

project_name: Transformation of Schoolyards to Combat Climate Change Effects and Promote Healthy and Active Environments
topic_keywords: education | capacity | methodology | toolkit | pilot action | pilot actions | students
project_summary:
The escalating effects of climate change pose significant threats to cities; in particular, heavy rainfall, flooding, rising extreme temperatures, and urban heat island effects significantly impact the urban environment, life quality, and health in the Mediterranean. The Re-Act Schools project addresses these challenges by focusing on climate change adaptation and risk prevention in school outdoor spaces. Schoolyards, especially in cities where public green living areas are limited, are valuable assets that offer unique opportunities to test and promote Nature-based and innovative technological solutions for climate change mitigation and adaptation capable to reduce the climate hazard.

To achieve this goal, the project brings together a diverse and complementary partnership that comprises 2 research institutions, 5 public bodies, and a territorial development agency from 6 Mediterranean countries (Italy, Spain, France, Croatia, Bulgaria, and Cyprus), that will work together to transform 6 schoolyards through Pilot Actions into green, climate-resilient and innovative spaces that promote healthy and active lifestyles among students and the wider community, according to the goals of the New European Bauhaus and Green Deal Program.

The testing activity will be accompanied by a capacity-building phase finalised to define an innovative design methodology to mitigate heat stress, manage water resources efficiently, enhance biodiversity, and offer climate shelters to the citizens in the Mediterranean urban context based on the use of ENVI-met microclimatic simulations and Digital Urban Climate Twin models to test advanced methods for analysing, designing, and monitoring climate-resilient schoolyards.

The knowledge developed during the project will be collected in an innovative Action Plan and a multimedia tool to support the Public Administration of the Med Area in managing the regenerative transformation of schoolyards and public spaces.

project_name: Social Ecological Impact Assessment for the Maritime Sector in the Atlantic Area
partner_name: Direção Regional de Políticas Marítimas (Direção de Serviços da Biodiversidade e Política do Mar)
topic_keywords: methodology | methodologies | toolkit | knowledge exchange | uptake | sme | smes | platform
project_summary:
Impact4Mar aims to accelerate the transition of SMEs in the maritime sector of the Atlantic area by facilitating an innovative access to tools and methodologies for assessing Impact, Risks and Opportunities (IRO) in Corporate Social Responsibility (CSR) dimensions.
Through transnational knowledge exchange, Impact4Mar proposes an integrated approach based on a common methodology across the regional maritime ecosystems involved to: - Conduct an analysis of sustainability IROs assessment practices in the maritime sector - Prototype and validate a Toolkit through pilots in maritime SMEs -Capitalize and disseminate the results through a digital platform and bootcamps.
By offering maritime SME-focused solutions, the project empowers businesses to comply with EU sustainability regulations, align with the EU Green Deal and UN 2030 Agenda and enhance resilience, competitiveness and innovation as a catalyst for the Blue Economy’s sustainable development in Europe.

2.0, fostering sustainable and innovative enterprises.

project_summary:
DigiATLA drives digital transformation in Atlantic aquaculture by fostering capacity building in sustainable aquaculture and precision nutrition technologies. It tackles key challenges like limited digital integration, inefficiencies and environmental impacts, which hinder the sector's resilience and competitiveness.
By promoting the adoption of Industry

4.0, DIGIATLA enhances digital capacity, equipping the professionals with tools and knowledge to improve efficiency, reduce environmental impacts and boost sustainability. Key outputs include digital training programs with online repository, pilot demonstrations and awareness initiatives on Industry

4.0 adoption. Beneficiaries are aquaculture professionals, researchers and educational communities.
Through a transnational approach DigiATLA merges expertise from multiple regions to align strategies, share best practices and develop scalable, impactful and broad solutions for a sustainable and competitive Atlantic aquaculture sector.

project_summary:
PROJECT TYPE: 1 - Thematic projects
~
MAIN INTERVENTION FIELD:

160. Measures to improve the accessibility, effectiveness and resilience of healthcare systems (excluding infrastructure)
~
SUMMARY: SALAM project, led by Polyclinic of Bari, brings together technological universities from Italy (POLIBA), Spain (University of Barcelona), Jordan (JUST), and Lebanon (University of Balamand), and the two Caritas from Lebanon and Jordan, intertwining scientific-technological and humanistic-social demands. It addresses the crisis of public health systems in the countries involved in the project, expressed differently but with common aspects such as shortage of healthcare providers and resources,impacting on equity of access to health.
Human complexity requires a more holistic approach to healthcare, including highly specialised, competent, flexible, resilient, and cooperative healthcare professionalism.
Within Open Innovation methodological framework, which underlies synergies between different, complementary disciplines, SALAM will develop a comprehensive solution that will bring mutual benefits to involved countries, envisaging:
Two hospital hubs in Lebanon and Jordan, integrated with telecardiology service, Helis Infarct.NET platform and related equipment, in use at the Polyclinic of Bari, which will be scaled up and uptaken with the support of academic partners. The hubs will include Medical Mobile Units and will contribute to making right to health concrete.
Innovative sanitaire protocols for medical staff training and for raising awareness among caregivers and local populations, to ensure the functioning of the HUBs, promote knowledge exchange and empower communities on new medical practices.
Transnational Research Center among partner universities on both sides of Mediterranean, which, moving from health needs of territories, will work together to develop accessible medical bio-devices, benefiting everyone - especially those affected by CVDs - so that "no one is left behind" and the value of inclusion can find substance across different nations, in a relationship of mutual enrichment where peace will be direct consequence of well-being for all.
~
KEEP KEYWORDS: Health and social services, ICT and digital society, Innovation capacity and awareness-raising

project_name: Replenishing the sMall wAter cyclE towards a more resilieNt Agriculture
programme: 2021 - 2027 Interreg VI-B NEXT Mediterranean Sea Basin (NEXT MED)
topic_keywords: training | skills | capacity building | capacity | communication | reuse | sme | communities
project_summary:
PROJECT TYPE: 1 - Thematic projects
~
MAIN INTERVENTION FIELD: 064. Water management and water resource conservation (including river basin management, specific climate change adaptation measures, reuse, leakage reduction)
~
SUMMARY: The disruption of natural water cycles caused by human activities, such as land use changes, agricultural practices, and urbanisation, is leading to ecological damage in the Mediterranean (Med) region which is particularly vulnerable to the impacts of global warming. Despite this, water management policies often focus on large-scale infrastructure projects, such as dams and water treatment plants that do not adequately consider the importance of Small Water Cycles (SWC). This disconnect has negative consequences as SWCs play a critical role in maintaining healthy ecosystems and supporting agricultural practices.
Additionally, it appears that there is limited knowledge among farming communities on the concept of restoring SWCs and its benefits, including conserving water sources, improving soil health and increasing agricultural productivity.
The Med region offers a suitable context to address these global challenges at a regional scale and serves as a real test bed for identifying effective solutions. In this respect, MAENA is designed to build capacities and support the adoption of practices targeting SWC restoration to improve on farm water use efficiency. The project will be implemented based on a participatory approach while actively involving farmers and surrounding local communities.
Four demonstration farms will provide proof of concept of SWC restoration that will be deployed in twenty farmers’ lands in partner countries.
MAENA will transfer knowledge and skills on SWC restoration to the targeted community through transnational training and capacity building with onsite, online and peer exchanges formats.
MAENA will form the first core of a Hub fostering SWC restoration in the Med region, enabling decision makers to replicate and scale-up the project outcomes, and carry forward this new paradigm.
This approach will result in more resilient and sustainable farming communities supporting both people and the environment by replenishing our water (مائنا).
~
KEEP KEYWORDS: Agriculture and fisheries and forestry, Soil and air quality, Water management

project_name: Biodegradable aquaculture nets
topic_keywords: training | training materials | skills | capacity building | capacity | methodology | uptake | sme | communities
project_summary:
AquaBioNets is a project supporting sustainable aquaculture in six Mediterranean countries. It brings together key groups like governments, businesses, researchers, and communities to promote eco-friendly practices, especially the use of biodegradable nets.
The project strengthens skills, supports innovation, and helps create local policies through a collaborative approach called the Living Lab. It addresses issues like pollution, tough global competition, and unclear regulations. All results, including training materials and policy guides, will be shared publicly to help spread sustainable aquaculture across the region.

The project strengthens skills, supports innovation, and helps create local policies through a collaborative approach called the Living Lab. It addresses issues like pollution, tough global competition, and unclear regulations. All results, including training materials and policy guides, will be shared publicly to help spread sustainable aquaculture across the region.

project_name: Holistic EnvironmenTAl and Social Adaptation Framework for Extreme heat
topic_keywords: learning | education | capacity | methodology | awareness | communication | sme | communities
project_summary:
Cities often become much hotter during heatwaves because of concrete surfaces, lack of green spaces, and city design. Some communities are especially at risk, particularly in poorer neighborhoods where people may already face health and social challenges.

To tackle this, HEATSAFE is taking action in Spain, Italy, Albania, Bulgaria, and Greece. The project is helping cities create clear plans to prepare for and respond to heatwaves. It supports local governments in making smart decisions using practical tools, while also involving local people to make sure solutions match the needs of each area.

In each location, HEATSAFE is testing real-life improvements like cool areas for public use, better emergency responses, and education campaigns to raise awareness. The results will be shared widely so that other cities across the Mediterranean can use the same methods.

By combining science, urban planning, public health, and community input, HEATSAFE aims to build safer, healthier, and more climate-ready cities for everyone.

project_name: Towards a Mediterranean model of Regenerative Tourism as Climate Change adaptation and risk prevention
topic_keywords: training | academy | capacity building | capacity | sme | communities
project_summary:
More than 70% of European citizens say they are willing to change their travel habits to a more sustainable model. Regenera4MED wants to go further!
Its concept of Regenerative Tourism goes beyond sustainability, it actively seeks to restore and revitalize natural ecosystems, strengthen local cultures, and empower communities. It promotes climate change adaptation and resilience while fostering meaningful connections that generate long-term, shared value for both people and the planet.
Regenera4MED will be tested in 6 high-profile tourist destinations: Catalonia (Spain), Corsica (France), Liguria and Abruzzo (Italy), Split-Dalmatia (Croatia) and Crete (Greece). Its long-term goals? To reposition the Mediterranean as a global benchmark for Regenerative tourism and to promote the region as a destination for the tourism of the future.

project_summary: The INSPIRE project wants to transform agrobiodiversity conservation into touristic opportunities. Agrobiodiversity is the term used to describe the way natural ecosystems work in areas of farming activities.How can this be achieved? First, by getting the 8 partners working on designing a sustainable tourism model based on agrobiodiversity. In the end, all these initiatives would be transferable to a wider sample of territories in the future!

project_summary:
BEACon – Strategic Support for Atlantic Innovation and Business.
The BEACon Project will transform business support in the Atlantic Area, enhancing success rates through an evidence-based toolkit for business advisors. Using insights from Randomised Controlled Trials, BEACon ensures training and advisory services drive real impact.
Atlantic SMEs face hurdles in digital transformation, sustainability, and competitiveness. BEACon aligns business support with EU priorities, including the Green Deal and Atlantic Action Plan

2.0, fostering sustainable and innovative enterprises.
Key Outputs & Benefits: a scalable toolkit to optimise business training; pilot programmes tailored for Atlantic Area SMEs; economic impact analysis for continuous improvement. By leveraging transnational cooperation, BEACon will map a path to success for Atlantic SMEs and innovators.

project_name: Supporting the implementation of blue and circular tourism in the Mediterranean
partner_name: Association of the Mediterranean Chambers of Commerce and Industry
project_summary:
The RECIRCLE MED project for sustainable coastal tourism has two characteristics that make it stand out.
First, it integrates two approaches that are often considered separately: the circular economy, i.e. the reuse of resources, and the blue economy, i.e. the protection of coastal areas. How? By building on the tools and results of two previous successful projects: INCIRCLE and REBOOT MED.
Then, by bringing these approaches to smaller tourist destinations that do not have the capacity or funding to implement them. How? Through a step-by-step transfer process that facilitates their adoption and ensures the development of local plans tailored to their needs.

partner_name: Javna ustanova RERA S.D za koordinaciju i razvoj Splitsko-dalmatinske županije
topic_keywords: training | academy | capacity building | capacity | sme | communities
project_summary:
More than 70% of European citizens say they are willing to change their travel habits to a more sustainable model. Regenera4MED wants to go further!
Its concept of Regenerative Tourism goes beyond sustainability, it actively seeks to restore and revitalize natural ecosystems, strengthen local cultures, and empower communities. It promotes climate change adaptation and resilience while fostering meaningful connections that generate long-term, shared value for both people and the planet.
Regenera4MED will be tested in 6 high-profile tourist destinations: Catalonia (Spain), Corsica (France), Liguria and Abruzzo (Italy), Split-Dalmatia (Croatia) and Crete (Greece). Its long-term goals? To reposition the Mediterranean as a global benchmark for Regenerative tourism and to promote the region as a destination for the tourism of the future.

project_name: INSPIRE: INtegrating biodiverSity Preservation and agrobIodiversity valorisation In touRism managEment in the MED area
topic_keywords: training | academy | guidelines | communication | valorisation | platform | communities
project_summary: The INSPIRE project wants to transform agrobiodiversity conservation into touristic opportunities. Agrobiodiversity is the term used to describe the way natural ecosystems work in areas of farming activities.How can this be achieved? First, by getting the 8 partners working on designing a sustainable tourism model based on agrobiodiversity. It will then be tested in five regions, where public and private tourism groups, farmers, and local communities will work together to apply it. In the end, all these initiatives would be transferable to a wider sample of territories in the future!
specific_objective: RSO2.7. Enhancing protection and preservation of nature, biodiversity and green infrastructure, including in urban areas, and reducing all forms of pollution

RealUrbanGreen will test a new model in eight pilot sites to help communities and local authorities create greener, healthier, and more resilient spaces. project_summary:
PROJECT TYPE: 1 - Thematic projects
~
MAIN INTERVENTION FIELD:

028. Technology transfer and cooperation between enterprises, research centres and higher education sector
~
SUMMARY: The HERACLES Project aims to establish and strengthen a multi-actor research network between companies and universities, focusing on the application of AI to enhance predictive medicine and health crisis preparedness, addressing significant common challenges in the Mediterranean region and transforming healthcare, making it more efficient. The project promotes Open Innovation and Open Research paradigms, exploring AI's potential alongside legal and regulatory frameworks to ensure proper valorisation of research through intellectual property and technology transfer mechanisms. Key activities of the HERACLES Project include implementing a Pilot Project that develops collaboration schemes between stakeholders at a transnational level, facilitating the exchange of researchers and entrepreneurs. These schemes aim to foster interaction between the scientific research community and the health technology business sector, integrating various stakeholders to develop AI-based tools specialised in predictive medicine and crisis management. Additionally, the project will explore and develop innovative business models to transform scientific research results into tangible commercial opportunities. By addressing common healthcare challenges through a collaborative and innovative approach, the HERACLES Project promises substantial benefits. It aims to drive forward a new era of AI-enhanced medical care, ultimately improving healthcare systems across the Mediterranean region and ensuring a more robust response to health crises. The expected changes include improved patient outcomes, more efficient allocation of healthcare resources, and the integration of advanced AI technologies into everyday clinical practice, benefiting patients, healthcare providers, and the broader medical community.
~

project_name: Mediterranean initiative to REgenerate wastewater Sludges for Optimal Use towaRd a Circular Economy
programme: 2021 - 2027 Interreg VI-B NEXT Mediterranean Sea Basin (NEXT MED)
topic_keywords: capacity building | capacity | toolkit | guidelines | communication | reuse | beneficiaries | communities
project_summary:
PROJECT TYPE: 1 - Thematic projects
~
MAIN INTERVENTION FIELD:

066. Waste water collection and treatment compliant with energy efficiency criteria
~
SUMMARY: MedRESOURCE tackles the pressing transnational challenges of water scarcity, land degradation, and waste management in the Mediterranean region. Currently, despite existing standards, WW sludge is still inadequately managed, leading to environmental harm and missed opportunities for resource recovery. MedRESOURCE will develop and demonstrate four innovative solutions to reuse wastewater sludge to enhance soil fertility, reduce environmental pollution, and foster sustainable agricultural practices.
The solutions will be jointly developed by a partnership of 6 organizations from 4 countries: Palestine, Italy, Tunisia and Jordan, supported by institutional and local associated partners. In each country a Living Lab will be set up to further develop existing solutions and to demonstrate their value to local communities, farmers, authorities and policy makers.
The expected change from MedRESOURCE is profound: converting waste into valuable resources, the project will significantly improve soil quality, reduce the need for chemical fertilizers, and mitigate pollution. This will enhance agricultural productivity and resilience to climate change, benefiting local farmers, communities, and the environment.
Key outputs are:
-	4 Living Labs (LL), each demonstrating a specific reuse of WW sludge in agriculture
-	A capacity building programme carried out in each LL, reaching about 200 beneficiaries
-	4 international study visist to the LL, promoting replicability and best practice exchange and reaching from 60 to 120 beneficiaries
-	Guidelines for policies development, providing references and standards at EU and MED level to integrate and better adapt regulatory frameworks to the innovative potential of WW sludge reuse.
MedRESOURCE offers a compelling solution to critical environmental issues, providing tangible benefits and fostering sustainable development across the Mediterranean. Supporting this project means investing in a greener, more resilient future for the region.
~

project_name: EmpoweerNEET MED: Upskilling NEETs through Social and Solidarity Economy
programme: 2021 - 2027 Interreg VI-B NEXT Mediterranean Sea Basin (NEXT MED)
topic_keywords: training | learning | education | workshop | skills | upskilling | capacity | guidelines | handbook | dissemination | communication | entrepreneur | entrepreneurs | entrepreneurship | startup | platform | communities
project_summary:
PROJECT TYPE: 1 - Thematic projects
~
MAIN INTERVENTION FIELD:

151. Support for adult education (excluding infrastructure)
~
SUMMARY: EmpowerNEET is an innovative transnational initiative aimed at integrating NEETs—young people not engaged in employment, education, or training—into the Social and Solidarity Economy (SSE) across the Mediterranean. These individuals face significant challenges, including high unemployment rates, economic exclusion, and limited access to quality education and vocational training, exacerbating social inequalities. EmpowerNEET leverages the underutilised potential of the SSE to promote inclusive economic growth and social integration.

By facilitating the transnational sharing of best practices and expertise, EmpowerNEET brings together entities from Palestine, Spain, Greece, Tunisia, Lebanon, and France to effectively address NEET marginalisation. The project offers comprehensive training programs to develop entrepreneurial and employment skills, provides practical experiences through job shadowing and mentoring, and ensures sustainability via supportive policy frameworks and ethical financing models.

EmpowerNEET aims to transition NEETs from economic and social marginalisation to empowerment. Key outputs include an extensive capacity-building program that equips 300 NEETs with essential theoretical and practical skills tailored for the SSE. Additionally, the project collaborates with local authorities to develop sustainable financing strategies, securing the long-term success of SSE initiatives and providing systemic support for NEETs.

The expected change includes improved employability and entrepreneurial capacity among NEETs. This project will benefit NEETs by enhancing their skills and opportunities, local governments by providing robust frameworks for NEETs and SSE support, and the broader community by stimulating economic growth and social cohesion. EmpowerNEET not only enhances the economic prospects of NEETs but also strengthens local communities by supporting SSEs, boosting economic vitality, and fostering inclusive growth.
~

project_summary:
PROJECT TYPE: 1 - Thematic projects
~
MAIN INTERVENTION FIELD:

151. Support for adult education (excluding infrastructure)
~
SUMMARY: EmpowerNEET is an innovative transnational initiative aimed at integrating NEETs—young people not engaged in employment, education, or training—into the Social and Solidarity Economy (SSE) across the Mediterranean. These individuals face significant challenges, including high unemployment rates, economic exclusion, and limited access to quality education and vocational training, exacerbating social inequalities. EmpowerNEET leverages the underutilised potential of the SSE to promote inclusive economic growth and social integration.

By facilitating the transnational sharing of best practices and expertise, EmpowerNEET brings together entities from Palestine, Spain, Greece, Tunisia, Lebanon, and France to effectively address NEET marginalisation. The project offers comprehensive training programs to develop entrepreneurial and employment skills, provides practical experiences through job shadowing and mentoring, and ensures sustainability via supportive policy frameworks and ethical financing models.

EmpowerNEET aims to transition NEETs from economic and social marginalisation to empowerment. Key outputs include an extensive capacity-building program that equips 300 NEETs with essential theoretical and practical skills tailored for the SSE. Additionally, the project collaborates with local authorities to develop sustainable financing strategies, securing the long-term success of SSE initiatives and providing systemic support for NEETs.

The expected change includes improved employability and entrepreneurial capacity among NEETs. This project will benefit NEETs by enhancing their skills and opportunities, local governments by providing robust frameworks for NEETs and SSE support, and the broader community by stimulating economic growth and social cohesion. EmpowerNEET not only enhances the economic prospects of NEETs but also strengthens local communities by supporting SSEs, boosting economic vitality, and fostering inclusive growth.
~

project_summary:
PROJECT TYPE: 2 - Youth-oriented projects
~
MAIN INTERVENTION FIELD:

152. Measures to promote equal opportunities and active participation in society
~
SUMMARY: HorizonCraft is a 24 months project planned by a partnership composed by 6 partners based in 3 countries; Italy, Lebanon and Egypt.
The action tackles the joint challenges of the EU-MED region, particularly the growing number of young people who lack access to job opportunities. The primary issue identified is the mismatch between the skills of young people and the needs of the labor market, highlighting the need for both improved and new mechanisms to bridge this gap.
The project specifically targets young people with fewer opportunities and micro, small, and medium-sized enterprises (MSMEs). It aims to answer two critical questions:
What do youth want? Opportunities to learn, grow, and be integrated into the labor market.
What do companies want? Prepared talent ready to join them and contribute to sustainable growth.
We believe that a partnership between youth organizations and MSMEs is crucial to instill resilience in young people, equipping them with the competencies and skills needed for employment and self-employment.
The main outputs can be summed up in 5 blocks:
Effective outreach to the target group, leading to a good take-up rate: plans and guidelines to ensure continuity, stability and participation of participants in the project, especially youth coming from disadvantaged backgrounds.
Capacity building pilots in the 3 involved territories: 1) by providing learning models and environments enabling participants to develop skills relevant for the labour market for youth and 2) Strengthening the capacity of MSMEs to mentor and support youth in their growth and development process.
Matching platform: to facilitate the match of youngsters and companies based on the principle of demand/supply.
Internship placement: arrangements for skills development and activation in the labour market.
Policy recommendations and sustainability plans: To advocate on policy reforms and territorial cooperation across borders in the EU-MED region.
~

project_summary:
PROJECT TYPE: 1 - Thematic projects
~
MAIN INTERVENTION FIELD:

041. Energy efficiency renovation of existing housing stock, demonstration projects and supporting measures
~
SUMMARY: SHAREN, “Energy-SHARing solutions and communities for enhanced ENergy efficiency in Mediterranean historic centres”, addresses, through transnational cooperation, the Mediterranean challenge of the Energy Transition in Heritage urban contexts: while most of historic city centres’ buildings are highly energy inefficient, the installation of renewable energy is struggling due to their architectural and historical significance requiring a balance between modern energy transition and preservation.

SHAREN introduces 6 Energy Sharing Solutions (ESS), including Energy Communities (EC), to enhance green energy’s efficiency, secure its supply, empower citizens & reduce energy poverty across Spain, Lebanon, Türkiye, Italy & Jordan, preserving the historical beauty and value of heritage buildings and piloted centres according to the New European Bauhaus principles- .

4 of these Energy Sharing Solutions will be tested by SHAREN’s frontrunners: 2 RE cooperatives connected to the grid with peer-to-peer energy exchange (P2P) in Lebanon, 1 ESS with P2P project in Jordan, and a fully operational EC connected to the grid in Spain. In LB & JO, the pilots include battery storage to secure energy supply. These pilots serve as practical models for the SHAREN followers (Italy & Türkiye) developing their own solutions while learning from them.

SHAREN benefits the diversity of groups of actors -owners of public and private buildings, residents, employees & tourists- by improving energy costs and comfort through reliable energy provision. These efforts are guided by the SHAREN Protocol, which outlines steps for adapting and replicating ESS/ECs in other Med historic centres. An integral learning experience for uptakers -SHAREN Guide, Academy & Twinning- & a Long-term impact strategy culminating in a SHAREN Memorandum of Understanding will consolidate SHAREN’s reach and create a transnational network committed to advancing energy transition in Med historic centres.

~

project_summary:
PROJECT TYPE: 1 - Thematic projects
~
MAIN INTERVENTION FIELD:

067. Household waste management: prevention, minimisation, sorting, reuse, recycling measures
~
SUMMARY: In the Mediterranean, ORIENTATE tackles the challenges caused by climate change. It focusses on the agricultural sector, a vital artery of the region's livelihoods. In this region agriculture is vulnerable and sustainability is always under threat. ORIENTATE develops targeted solutions to waste streams generated by olive oil extraction and citrus fruit processing. Drawing inspiration from the innovative practices of pioneering Italian and Spanish partners, cutting-edge circular economy models are used to revolutionise waste management. ORIENTATE unfolds across Turkey, Jordan, and Lebanon. It mitigates environmental harm and aims to spark ripple effects of socio-economic empowerment. By harnessing agricultural waste, ORIENTATE opens the door to economic opportunities, job creation, and environmental stewardship, while enriching the lives of farmers, entrepreneurs, and communities alike. ORIENTATE also forges strategic alliances and fosters supportive regulatory frameworks, laying the groundwork for sustainable development and aligning seamlessly with the vision of a resilient, eco-conscious Europe and its neighbouring regions.
The project establishes three pilot labs in Jordan, Turkey, and Lebanon, each focussing on converting agricultural by-products like Citrus Pulp Depectinized (CPD) and Olive pomace into animal feed and cosmetics ingredients, promoting a circular economy. These labs will develop bioproducts from olive pomace for animal nutrition and cosmetics in Jordan and Lebanon, and transform CPD into animal feed in Turkey. Training materials and joint activities across these countries will educate at least 90 professionals in the citrus and olive supply chains on sustainable waste management practices. Additionally, six Memoranda of Understanding will be signed with stakeholders such as farmers, food industries, and public authorities to adopt these sustainable models and promote regional agricultural innovation, thus tackling unemployment.
~

project_summary:
PROJECT TYPE: 1 - Thematic projects
~
MAIN INTERVENTION FIELD:

023. Skills development for smart specialisation, industrial transition, entrepreneurship and adaptability of enterprises to change
~
SUMMARY: Med region, renowned for its cultural heritage & natural beauty, attracts millions of tourists annually, making tourism a critical sector for economic stability & growth. However, the vulnerability of tourist SMEs to crises, ranging from natural disasters & pandemics to economic fluctuations & political instability, poses significant challenges.
ReTour-Med is a forward-thinking project to revolutionize Mediterranean tourism sector by enhancing the resilience & competitiveness of tourist SMEs through technological innovation & sustainable practices.
Expected Change: By integrating advanced technologies like AI, IoT & blockchain, ReTour-Med transforms current reactive approach into a proactive, resilient framework. This shift improves crisis management & enhances overall tourist experience, ensuring that Med remains a top destination amidst evolving global challenges.
Main Outputs of ReTour-MED:

1.Innovation Hub: Fosters collaboration across sectors, boosting MSMEs' access to cutting-edge solutions & expertise

2.Tailored Technology Implementation Plans: Customized strategies for MSMEs to adopt & integrate new technologies

3.Case Studies & Evaluations: Assessments of pilot projects showcasing successful technology integrations

4.Training & Learning Platform: Elevates skills in digital tools & sustainable practices

5.Policy Recommendation Reports: Guides regional strategies for a robust tourism ecosystem
Primary beneficiaries are Med MSMEs in tourism, technology providers, policymakers, local communities& tourists, unemployed individuals, low-skilled employees & tourism staff.
ReTour-Med secures the future of Med tourism, making it more sustainable efficient & attractive. It offers a comprehensive response to region's shared vulnerabilities, promising significant ROI for stakeholders & contributing to global effort to sustain tourism as a force for economic & social good. ReTour-Med is an investment in the future prosperity & resilience of entire Med region.
~

project_name: Competitiveness and Internationalisation of Rural Tourism SMEs in the Mediterranean
programme: 2021 - 2027 Interreg VI-B NEXT Mediterranean Sea Basin (NEXT MED)
topic_keywords: training | skills | capacity building | capacity | methodologies | communication | sme | smes | entrepreneur | entrepreneurs | entrepreneurship | platform | communities
project_summary:
PROJECT TYPE: 1 - Thematic projects
~
MAIN INTERVENTION FIELD:

021. SME business development and internationalisation, including productive investments
~
SUMMARY: CReSInMed focuses on strengthening and improving the internationalisation capacities of tourism (M)SMEs in the Mediterranean rural areas which face unique challenges: they are small, uncompetitive and their remoteness from large urban centres reduces access to markets, limiting opportunities for growth. Human resources have limited access to training opportunities. CReSInMed actions contribute to increase the potential of these (M)SMEs and focuses on improving skills and entrepreneurship, while also contributing to networking and strengthening transnational cooperation in the Mediterranean. The changes expected from the project compared to the existing situation is to increase competitiveness in regional, national and international markets of (M)SMEs, improve the related skills, and strengthen networking capacities of operators. 50 (M)SMEs enhance their knowledge with specific training programmes supported by a task force of experts. Topics related to productivity, innovation, considering the importance of digital transition and sustainability are addressed. Networking among operators is the basis for exchanging experiences, developing joint activities to make internationalisation sustainable.
(M)SMEs can count on technological support to facilitate their work through the use of a digital toolbox that complements capacity building initiatives by providing tools to support (M)SMEs' growth and internationalisation efforts.
The actions taken generate multiplier effects that extend to the sector concerned and the community. The solutions implemented by CReSInMed, in particular cluster, MoU and the platform, can continue to produce benefits after the end of the project. (M)SMEs, local communities, public authorities, stakeholders receive long-term benefits from project implementation, with improved socio-economic conditions. CReSInMed positively contributes to the transformation of the tourism and economic landscape of the rural regions involved.
~

SUMMARY: CReSInMed focuses on strengthening and improving the internationalisation capacities of tourism (M)SMEs in the Mediterranean rural areas which face unique challenges: they are small, uncompetitive and their remoteness from large urban centres reduces access to markets, limiting opportunities for growth. Human resources have limited access to training opportunities. CReSInMed actions contribute to increase the potential of these (M)SMEs and focuses on improving skills and entrepreneurship, while also contributing to networking and strengthening transnational cooperation in the Mediterranean. The changes expected from the project compared to the existing situation is to increase competitiveness in regional, national and international markets of (M)SMEs, improve the related skills, and strengthen networking capacities of operators. 50 (M)SMEs enhance their knowledge with specific training programmes supported by a task force of experts. Topics related to productivity, innovation, considering the importance of digital transition and sustainability are addressed. Networking among operators is the basis for exchanging experiences, developing joint activities to make internationalisation sustainable.
(M)SMEs can count on technological support to facilitate their work through the use of a digital toolbox that complements capacity building initiatives by providing tools to support (M)SMEs' growth and internationalisation efforts.
The actions taken generate multiplier effects that extend to the sector concerned and the community. The solutions implemented by CReSInMed, in particular cluster, MoU and the platform, can continue to produce benefits after the end of the project. (M)SMEs, local communities, public authorities, stakeholders receive long-term benefits from project implementation, with improved socio-economic conditions. CReSInMed positively contributes to the transformation of the tourism and economic landscape of the rural regions involved.
~

project_summary:
PROJECT TYPE: 1 - Thematic projects
~
MAIN INTERVENTION FIELD:

067. Household waste management: prevention, minimisation, sorting, reuse, recycling measures
~
SUMMARY: THE PROBLEM
Growing consumption is generating changes in the composition of waste, including 'new' waste streams such as electronic. The Mediterranean Strategy for Sustainable Development 2016-2025 is based on the principle that economic growth needs to be harmonized with the protection of natural resources.

In EU countries, the WEEE is managed within a strict legislative framework, however, only one third of the WEEE produced is collected separately and destined for appropriate treatment.

Furthermore, countries not belonging to EU territories have a less restrictive WEEE waste management legislation, contributing to improver disposal and treatment methods of discarded electrical equipment.

E-waste exposes a high environmental risk for NEXT MED area, as informal recycling, and e-waste abandoned in dumping sites, provide hazardous substances seed into groundwater, affecting water sources, aquifers and sea water.

THE OBJECTIVE
WEEE VALUE aims to promote local and regional policies for better e-waste management, to reduce WEEE environmental damage in the selected areas, while promoting:
Comprehensive awareness campaigns, through the solution WEEE AWARE,
New innovative eco-business models and an efficient online collection platform, through the solution WEEE COLLECT,
Social inclusion in WEEE management companies, through the solution WEEE SOCIAL.

TRANSNATIONAL COOPERATION
The unification of waste management criteria in the transnational MED area is essential, since otherwise it could facilitate the dynamization of the illegal movement of e-waste from areas with stricter regulations towards areas with more permissive regulations. To stop this threat, it is necessary to take into account common joint planning, and coordinated business promotion. WEEE VALUE gathers 8 partners and 5 associated, covering 8 countries, which will approve 8 new Action Plans for e-waste management.
~
