project_name: Scaling-up advanced lentiviral and CAR-T academic production across the Mediterranean region
programme: 2021 - 2027 Interreg VI-B NEXT Mediterranean Sea Basin (NEXT MED)
topic_keywords: education | capacity | technology transfer | dissemination | communication | uptake | enterprise | enterprises | companies | beneficiaries
project_summary:
PROJECT TYPE: 1 - Thematic projects
~
MAIN INTERVENTION FIELD:

028. Technology transfer and cooperation between enterprises, research centres and higher education sector
~
SUMMARY: Chimeric Antigen Receptor T-cell therapy (CAR-T) is an immunotherapy used to treat certain cancers. It involves modifying the patient’s own T-cells to better recognize and attack cancer cells in the body. T-cells are collected from the patient’s blood and engineered to produce a Chimeric Antigen Receptor (CAR) on their surface. This genetic modification is usually performed using a lentiviral vector (LV), carrying the genetic material that will be introduced into the T-cells. Once modified, the T-cells are infused back into the patient, with the new ability to recognize specific antigens found on cancer cells surface and destroy them.

Our project addresses the challenge of limited access to CAR T-cell therapy by fostering transnational collaboration across EU Mediterranean Countries (EUMC, Spain and Italy) and Mediterranean Partner Countries (MPC, Lebanon and Jordan). Through our concerted efforts, we aim to democratize access to CAR T-cell therapy in the Mediterranean region. To this end, we will increase LV production capacity in Spain, ensuring a stable and ample supply of vectors and overcoming a major bottleneck in therapy production. Spain will share its LV technology with Italy, Lebanon and Jordan, that will validate cell production in their respective facilities.

The final beneficiaries of our project are cancer patients across EUMC and MPC, who will directly benefit from improved access to CAR T-cell therapy, offering them a lifeline in their battle against cancer. Additionally, healthcare providers, researchers, and pharmaceutical companies involved in cancer research and treatment will benefit from enhanced collaboration, ultimately leading to advancements in the field and better outcomes for patients.

programme: 2021 - 2027 Interreg VI-B NEXT Mediterranean Sea Basin (NEXT MED)

project_summary:
PROJECT TYPE: 1 - Thematic projects
~
MAIN INTERVENTION FIELD:

028. Technology transfer and cooperation between enterprises, research centres and higher education sector
~
SUMMARY: Chimeric Antigen Receptor T-cell therapy (CAR-T) is an immunotherapy used to treat certain cancers. It involves modifying the patient’s own T-cells to better recognize and attack cancer cells in the body. T-cells are collected from the patient’s blood and engineered to produce a Chimeric Antigen Receptor (CAR) on their surface. This genetic modification is usually performed using a lentiviral vector (LV), carrying the genetic material that will be introduced into the T-cells. Once modified, the T-cells are infused back into the patient, with the new ability to recognize specific antigens found on cancer cells surface and destroy them.

Our project addresses the challenge of limited access to CAR T-cell therapy by fostering transnational collaboration across EU Mediterranean Countries (EUMC, Spain and Italy) and Mediterranean Partner Countries (MPC, Lebanon and Jordan). Through our concerted efforts, we aim to democratize access to CAR T-cell therapy in the Mediterranean region. To this end, we will increase LV production capacity in Spain, ensuring a stable and ample supply of vectors and overcoming a major bottleneck in therapy production. Spain will share its LV technology with Italy, Lebanon and Jordan, that will validate cell production in their respective facilities.

The final beneficiaries of our project are cancer patients across EUMC and MPC, who will directly benefit from improved access to CAR T-cell therapy, offering them a lifeline in their battle against cancer. Additionally, healthcare providers, researchers, and pharmaceutical companies involved in cancer research and treatment will benefit from enhanced collaboration, ultimately leading to advancements in the field and better outcomes for patients.

After the project, we expect a transformed landscape: improved access, accelerated adoption, and enhanced collaboration. Patients will benefit from expanded treatment options and researchers will continue pushing the boundaries of CAR T-cell therapy.
~

SUMMARY: SheEmpower proposal stands as a beacon of opportunity within the NEXT MED program. With a focus on empowering women-led businesses, SheEmpower aims to address the joint challenges faced at a transnational level, such as limited access to resources, digitalization challenges, and the lack of entrepreneurs skills and commercialization support.
SheEmpower build on three pillars Empowering Women’s Skills, Business and Innovation. The diverse and transnational consortium including chambers of commerce, universities, and business networks across Cyprus, Italy, Spain, France, Egypt and Lebanon, ensures a robust support system and proven capacity to deliver the goals.
Empowering Women’s Skills through transformative change by providing entrepreneurship capacity-building, mentorship programs to enhance the competitiveness. Empowering Women’s Business through e-business and digital transformation capacity building, and SheEmpower MED e-Store creation which is dedicated to commercialize products and services. project_summary:
PROJECT TYPE: 1 - Thematic projects
~
MAIN INTERVENTION FIELD: 028. Technology transfer and cooperation between enterprises, research centres and higher education sector
~
SUMMARY: PROTECHMED seeks to establish an enabling ecosystem for co-designing and testing five eco-innovative solutions for horticultural greenhouses. These solutions are integral to the digital and innovation transition within the ecological context. With a strong focus on water and energy conservation in horticultural crops, the ultimate aim is to drive the transition toward a more intelligent and sustainable business model for greenhouse food production.
Key aspects include:

1. Agri-Cluster Alliance: more than 300 among Researchers, professionals, institutions, and horticultural companies collaborate across Greece, Italy, Malta, Tunisia, Lebanon, and Turkey. Their goal is to enhance agronomic and technological expertise among MSMEs and bridge the gap between research and agricultural companies along the value chain.

2. Smart Eco-Innovative Technologies: These technologies improve water and nutrient efficiency, incorporate photovoltaic installations, and include a Decision Support System for overall efficiency monitoring. Remote assistance via augmented reality (AR) visors and soilless techniques for closed-loop crop production are also explored.

3. Capacity Development: Training programs for at least 150 technicians and agronomists, along with coaching for MSMEs adopting PROTECHMED Solutions.

4. Inception Camp: Held during the project’s initial phase, it involves on-field visits and focus groups. Challenges are addressed collaboratively by the transnational Living Lab, which engages stakeholders from the quadruple helix (academia, industry, government, and civil society).

5. Joint Business Model: In the final project phase, public consultations contribute to a shared Action Plan and RoadMap, committing stakeholders to transition toward a smarter business model for sustainable food production.
~

project_summary:
PROJECT TYPE: 1 - Thematic projects
~
MAIN INTERVENTION FIELD: 028. Technology transfer and cooperation between enterprises, research centres and higher education sector
~
SUMMARY: PROTECHMED seeks to establish an enabling ecosystem for co-designing and testing five eco-innovative solutions for horticultural greenhouses. These solutions are integral to the digital and innovation transition within the ecological context. With a strong focus on water and energy conservation in horticultural crops, the ultimate aim is to drive the transition toward a more intelligent and sustainable business model for greenhouse food production.
Key aspects include:

1. Agri-Cluster Alliance: more than 300 among Researchers, professionals, institutions, and horticultural companies collaborate across Greece, Italy, Malta, Tunisia, Lebanon, and Turkey. Their goal is to enhance agronomic and technological expertise among MSMEs and bridge the gap between research and agricultural companies along the value chain.

2. Smart Eco-Innovative Technologies: These technologies improve water and nutrient efficiency, incorporate photovoltaic installations, and include a Decision Support System for overall efficiency monitoring. Remote assistance via augmented reality (AR) visors and soilless techniques for closed-loop crop production are also explored.

3. Capacity Development: Training programs for at least 150 technicians and agronomists, along with coaching for MSMEs adopting PROTECHMED Solutions.

4. Inception Camp: Held during the project’s initial phase, it involves on-field visits and focus groups. Challenges are addressed collaboratively by the transnational Living Lab, which engages stakeholders from the quadruple helix (academia, industry, government, and civil society).

5. Joint Business Model: In the final project phase, public consultations contribute to a shared Action Plan and RoadMap, committing stakeholders to transition toward a smarter business model for sustainable food production.
~

project_name: Towards the decarbonization of tile industry through an innovative semi-wet granulated technology for porcelain tiles
programme: 2021 - 2027 Interreg VI-B NEXT Mediterranean Sea Basin (NEXT MED)
partner_name: Çanakkale Teknopark Teknoloji Geliştirme Bölgesi A.Ş.
topic_keywords: workshop | capacity | technology transfer | awareness | awareness raising | communication | uptake | replication | enterprise | enterprises | platform | professionals | students
project_summary:
PROJECT TYPE: 1 - Thematic projects
~
MAIN INTERVENTION FIELD: 029. Research and innovation processes, technology transfer and cooperation between enterprises, research centres and universities, focusing on the low carbon economy, resilience and adaptation to climate change
~
SUMMARY: INNOVATILE aims at scaling up an innovative semi-wet granulation technology for porcelain tiles in order to create a more sustainable process for ceramic tile manufacturing which is an energy and resource intensive process. With the help of this technology and in alignment with UNSDG and European Green Deal policies, INNOVATILE expects to reduce energy demand by 30-35%, raw materials and water consumption by 10-20%, and greenhouse gas emissions by 30-35%. Additionally, it will allow to replace feldspar which is a critical raw material in the EU.

Within the project, after validation research, a pilot implementation will be performed with an end user then followed by replication in another country. Environmental analysis and comparisons with traditional manufacturing processes will be conducted. The developed inventory of secondary raw materials (SRMs) will be beneficial for not only tile manufacturers but also for other industries by providing information on locally available SRMs.

The HypocarbonTileTech network, a pioneering initiative on decarbonization, and its platform will be established to foster collaboration among stakeholders which are all actors of the value chain. The network's activities aim to raise awareness and initiate collaboration on decarbonization, renewables, circular economy, and waste management. These efforts will target various audiences, starting from students and researchers to industry professionals. Additionally, policy documents will be generated and disseminated to public authorities to ensure a long lasting and sustainable impact of the project results and the network.

The Interreg area represents one of the world's main ceramic tile producing regions, hosting four of the top ten largest producers: Spain, Italy, Türkiye, and Egypt. The impact of INNOVATILE’s technology will achieve a wider effect as these four countries are represented in the consortium, enabling INNOVATILE to engage with the world’s largest tile manufacturers
~

The developed inventory of secondary raw materials (SRMs) will be beneficial for not only tile manufacturers but also for other industries by providing information on locally available SRMs.

The HypocarbonTileTech network, a pioneering initiative on decarbonization, and its platform will be established to foster collaboration among stakeholders which are all actors of the value chain. The network's activities aim to raise awareness and initiate collaboration on decarbonization, renewables, circular economy, and waste management. These efforts will target various audiences, starting from students and researchers to industry professionals. Additionally, policy documents will be generated and disseminated to public authorities to ensure a long lasting and sustainable impact of the project results and the network.

The Interreg area represents one of the world's main ceramic tile producing regions, hosting four of the top ten largest producers: Spain, Italy, Türkiye, and Egypt. The impact of INNOVATILE’s technology will achieve a wider effect as these four countries are represented in the consortium, enabling INNOVATILE to engage with the world’s largest tile manufacturers
~

project_summary:
PROJECT TYPE: 1 - Thematic projects
~
MAIN INTERVENTION FIELD: 041. Energy efficiency renovation of existing housing stock, demonstration projects and supporting measures
~
SUMMARY: The Mediterranean region faces a severe climate crisis and unemployment. The region, being blessed with abundant solar energy, must form effective international partnerships to promptly transit to greener, low-carbon and resilient economies. Industry contributes significantly to global warming of the region, which is estimated to be 20% higher than the rest of the world. Decarbonized and self-sufficient industries could promote sustainable development by alleviating unemployment rates. With 8 partners from Italy, Spain, France, Türkiye, Palestine and Egypt and 9 Associated partners, GREENOLIVE is committed to build a novel solar energy pilot partially addressing process heat of the plastics industry, which must undergo a deep energy transformation. The solar system will rely on 600 kWe PV system to provide heat at about 300°C through electric heaters using the Power to Heat (P2H) concept. Using P2H concept for process heat at 300°C is a novel approach. It is expected to decrease fossil fuel use in the plastics plant by 18-22%, through using solar energy efficiently. When solar energy is in excess, packed-bed thermal energy storage (TES), scaled-up based on previous EU project results, will store heat for periods when radiation is unavailable. Integrating TES in the system will avoid using expensive batteries and make it more energy efficient at lower cost. Locally available waste materials will be developed as TES materials. Low-cost and locally available components will be used in the system to deliver affordable clean energy and contribute to UN SDG7. The project will also prepare a replication plan for textiles and agri-products industries and the residential sector. The creation of GREENOLIVE Training Kit and white paper will optimize knowledge and skills transfer in solar energy systems and policy improvement, benefiting local industries, society, education, and engineering companies.
~

project_summary:
PROJECT TYPE: 1 - Thematic projects
~
MAIN INTERVENTION FIELD: 145. Support for the development of digital skills
~
SUMMARY: In Europe and the MENA region, the underemployment and segregation of women to specific labour sectors as well as discrimination in the labour market result in a gender employment and wage gap (Women’s Empowerment in the Mediterranean Region). In self-employment, the global percentage of women who are entrepreneurs is 5.3% in Europe and 4.5% in the MENA region, below the 6,2% global percentage, mainly because of difficulties in accessing business support services and networks (Intergovernmental Monitoring Mechanism on Gender Equality).
The gender digital divide affects both the access and the use of the ICTs, as well as the number of female ICT graduates and experts working in the sector that in 2021 was only 19% (Education area of the EU commission). This gap is even bigger when it comes to vulnerable women facing additional barriers in the labour market.
DigiWAVE will encourage versatile employment (i.e. employment and self-employment) of vulnerable women in the digital economy and will enhance women’s labour and economic inclusion contributing to closing the gender digital divide in the region. It will create favourable conditions for a sustainable growth of women’s employability, on one side by providing training and support for acquiring new skills aligned with the digital global market, and on the other side by advocacy and dialogue between key stakeholders to generate concrete action plans, enhancing laboral prospection, visibility and networking.
The main expected outputs are as follows:

1. Competence development in ICT, empowerment and business development

2. Enhanced laboral inclusion through advocacy, prospection and networking for employability

3. Communication and visibility on the laboral and social inclusion of vulnerable women
The direct beneficiaries will be 200 vulnerable (at laboral, social and economic levels) women from Spain, Greece, Tunisia and Jordan, while others will benefit from resources. Engagement with 400 stakeholders is foreseen.
~

SUMMARY: YIELD aims at addressing the pressing challenges faced in the EUROMED region by young entrepreneurs targeting deep tech, and especially in the Blue, Green, circular economy and eco-innovation (BGCE) sectors by jointly establishing a comprehensive support system tailored to their unique needs.
BGCE sectors are increasingly important since they can foster innovation, stimulate regional economic development, and align EUROMED region with EU and global policies.
One common challenge faced by young entrepreneurs is the lack of access to tailored support and resources to help them turn their innovative ideas into successful businesses.

This innovative route involves creating transnational challenge-based networks for knowledge exchange, offer of specialized training and mentoring, facilitating connections with experienced mentors who can provide valuable guidance, insights, and access to wider networks.
The main output of YIELD is to empower at least 50 teams of young aspiring entrepreneurs, exploiting deep tech solutions to solve selected BGCE challenges (e.g. water scarcity, wildfires, waste management) in establishing their own micro enterprise and contribute to the growth and development of the entrepreneurship ecosystem in the EUROMED region.
YIELD capitalizes its joint transnational methodology on previous project results & best practices (from MED and EU) that support young entrepreneurship, from access to resources to fostering a supportive community, thus cultivating a vibrant ecosystem where young aspiring entrepreneurs can maximise their full potential and catalyze change.

project_summary:
PROJECT TYPE: 1 - Thematic projects
~
MAIN INTERVENTION FIELD: 023. Skills development for smart specialisation, industrial transition, entrepreneurship and adaptability of enterprises to change
~
SUMMARY: CERES objective is to establish Centers of Excellence (CoEs) in three project partner areas, in Greece, Palestine and Lebanon. CoEs will serve as innovation hubs, offering state-of-the-art training, fostering entrepreneurship and employment. Establishment of the CοEs will enable entrepreneurs, MSMEs, researchers, and graduates to receive interdisciplinary training from international teams of experts and obtain sufficient mentoring and technical support.The objective of the CoEs is to provide targeted capacity building that develops the skills and competencies required to meet the evolving needs of the market, especially in green and digital transition technologies. The Research Institutions (LP, P3) will develop small, flexible, online and/or physical presence courses offering cutting-edge knowledge in a specialised subject accompanied by practical experience as well for integrated support and networking to scale-up their project ideas. Practical knowledge will gain by the establishment and operation of Living Labs on smart and resilient energy solutions (WP3) with the support additionally of P1,P2,P5, for the business development of project ideas, as well as the configuration of the pilot projects (P3,WP4) to be developed by the project. Pilots will be based on different application technologies; they will embed an open-source management and supervisory system and collaborative platform, and they will be developed to be cost-effective, green and resilient (WP5). All partners will collaborate for the development of the pilot projects combining their expertise and offering a mix of state-of-the-art technology together with practical training and problem solving. WP6, clustering promotion, networking and a virtual collaborative platform and business model will ensure the sustainability of the outputs. Important aspect of CERES will be the effort to follow the NEB principles in planning and implementing the pilot projects, towards aesthetics, inclusivity, sustainabil
~

project_summary:
PROJECT TYPE: 1 - Thematic projects
~
MAIN INTERVENTION FIELD: 026. Support for innovation clusters including between businesses, research organisations and public authorities and business networks primarily benefiting SMEs
~
SUMMARY: CLUSTERATLAS4Med is the Mediterranean meta-cluster for rural tourism. Following a hub-and-Spoke model, ATLAS4MED will boost the competitiveness of tourism SMEs in rural areas through better and bespoke services useful to address the challenges of the digital and green transition, fostering interconnections among the relevant stakeholders of the quadruple-helix in the Mediterranean area.
For Ancient Greeks, Atlas was a giant so strong to carry the weight of the world on his shoulders. In Berber culture, Atlas refers to the main mountain range of North Africa. In Latin, the Atlas has come to mean book of maps. Atlas can thus be associated with meanings such as "strength", "support" and "orientation", reflecting the ATLAS4Med meta-cluster’s objective of developing a strategic approach to guide rural tourism actors across the NEXT MED area toward more sustainable growth.
The main novelty of our project will be the creation of a strategic trans-Mediterranean meta-cluster that will specifically support rural tourism, instead of tourism in general. Rural tourism can play a leading role for the sustainable growth of rural areas but it needs a different approach. Rural SMEs are, on average, less innovative and digitalised. This can prevent them from grasping the benefits of the global markets. Our trans-regional meta-cluster, closely aligned with the Eurocluster Rural Tourism funded under the Single Market Programme, will create synergies among the different actors and territories to sustain the long term growth of rural tourism while addressing the twin transition. The target groups to be involved are: R&D Organisations, VET providers, public administrations, Chambers of Commerce, Business organisations and rural associations.
8 full partners and 6 associated partners from 7 NextMED countries will work together to capitalise and share their experiences gained through other EU-funded projects and to create a new and durable trans-mediterranean cluster.
~

project_summary:
PROJECT TYPE: 1 - Thematic projects
~
MAIN INTERVENTION FIELD: 075. Support to environmentally-friendly production processes and resource efficiency in SMEs
~
SUMMARY: Waste2Fashion aims to help Mediterranean countries transition to a circular and resource-efficient economy by minimising the negative environmental impact of post-consumer textile waste and maximising the socio-economic opportunities presented by the growing sustainable fashion sector. The project will deliver two pilots to 1) collect, re-use and recycle and 2) to prevent and revalorise post-consumer textile waste.

The post-consumer textile waste management and treatment pilot (WP3) will address the shared challenges of Egypt, Italy, Lebanon, Spain, and Tunisia: adequate collection and sorting systems to meet the scale of need presented by a linear ‘fast’ fashion industry and the technical challenge of fibre separation in recycling. The holistic model, adapted to context, will include collection infrastructure; the transfer of leading-edge textile sorting technology; and the development of sustainable local value chains for the commercial exploitation of the treated materials.

The waste reduction and revalorisation pilot (WP4) aims to increase the ability of designers and start-ups to reduce waste in the design phase and to develop circular initiatives to revalorise recycled materials. The cross-border Eco-design Academy will build the capacity of 60 designers to contribute to waste-reduction in the design phase and to develop a circular business model. The cross-border Acceleration Lab will support the development of 10 economic initiatives that make use of recycled materials in their product design, including those produced by the waste management pilot, optimising industrial symbiosis. Initiatives will be supported with a range of business support services, including access to finance.

The holistic project model, including results and learnings, alongside policy recommendations will be disseminated across the region (WP2 & WP5), providing an actionable solution for policymakers and sector leaders to address the impacts of post-consumer textile waste.
~

SUMMARY: Waste2Fashion aims to help Mediterranean countries transition to a circular and resource-efficient economy by minimising the negative environmental impact of post-consumer textile waste and maximising the socio-economic opportunities presented by the growing sustainable fashion sector. The project will deliver two pilots to 1) collect, re-use and recycle and 2) to prevent and revalorise post-consumer textile waste.

The post-consumer textile waste management and treatment pilot (WP3) will address the shared challenges of Egypt, Italy, Lebanon, Spain, and Tunisia: adequate collection and sorting systems to meet the scale of need presented by a linear ‘fast’ fashion industry and the technical challenge of fibre separation in recycling. The holistic model, adapted to context, will include collection infrastructure; the transfer of leading-edge textile sorting technology; and the development of sustainable local value chains for the commercial exploitation of the treated materials.

The waste reduction and revalorisation pilot (WP4) aims to increase the ability of designers and start-ups to reduce waste in the design phase and to develop circular initiatives to revalorise recycled materials. The cross-border Eco-design Academy will build the capacity of 60 designers to contribute to waste-reduction in the design phase and to develop a circular business model. The cross-border Acceleration Lab will support the development of 10 economic initiatives that make use of recycled materials in their product design, including those produced by the waste management pilot, optimising industrial symbiosis. Initiatives will be supported with a range of business support services, including access to finance.

The holistic project model, including results and learnings, alongside policy recommendations will be disseminated across the region (WP2 & WP5), providing an actionable solution for policymakers and sector leaders to address the impacts of post-consumer textile waste.
~

project_summary:
PROJECT TYPE: 1 - Thematic projects
~
MAIN INTERVENTION FIELD: 140. Support for labour market matching and transitions
~
SUMMARY: Le projet EUSEEDS (Enable, Upgrade and Spread Employability, Entrepreneurship and Digital Skills) vise à déployer 20 centres universitaires dans 5 pays est-méditerranéens pour développer l’employabilité et les compétences numériques de la jeunesse.
Les pays méditerranéens concernés partagent nombre de défis communs telles qu’une jeunesse éprouvant des difficultés à s’insérer sur le marché de l’emploi formel. Cet enjeu de stabilité est imputable à une massification de l’accès à l’enseignement supérieur couplée à une inadéquation entre formations universitaires et besoins en main d’œuvre, qui crée un déficit généralisé d’employabilité. Le potentiel de création de richesse par le numérique reste trop réduit.
Proposé par un consortium équilibré et solide réunissant des partenaires de référence (Chypre, Égypte, France, Jordanie, Liban, Palestine), le projet, qui vise un impact massif en termes de personnes formées (7100 bénéficiaires finaux dans le cadre du projet, 4100 ensuite en flux annuel, objectifs atteignables), prévoit une réponse collective à des défis communs, sur la base d’initiatives déjà éprouvées, mises à l’échelle, pour une meilleure insertion professionnelle, en améliorant l’employabilité d’étudiants et jeunes diplômés, en développant leurs compétences professionnelles en numérique, leurs compétences transversales et l’auto-entrepreneuriat.
Les étudiants et jeunes diplômés ne seront pas les seuls bénéficiaires finaux. Les centres établis cibleront des publics spécifiques vulnérables au sein de leur communauté comme des jeunes sans emploi, en proposant des formations adaptées et à distance.
Le modèle consiste à équiper et doter des compétences nécessaires 20 centres de ressources universitaires pérennes situés dans des zones à fort enjeu social des 5 pays, dont la mission sera de fournir durablement des services internes (formations, préincubation de projets) et externes vers la communauté (formations à la littéracie et à la professionnalisation numérique).
~
KEEP KEYWORDS: Education and training, ICT and digital society, Labour market and employment
specific_objective: RSO4.2. Improving equal access to inclusive and quality services in education, training and lifelong learning through developing accessible infrastructure, including by fostering resilience for distance and on-line education and training
outputs:
(1.1) Les instruments de pilotage, gestion, partage et redevabilité sont mis en place et en oeuvre(EN)

(1.2) Des mécanismes de suivi et d’évaluation sont mis en place(EN)

(1.3) Un manuel de procédures financières est élaboré(EN)

(1.4) Un environnement collaboratif de travail est élaboré(EN)

(2.1) Les outils de communication sont mis en place(EN)

(2.2) Le projet est valorisé et rendu visible (presse écrite, audiovisuelle et réseaux sociaux)(EN)

(3.1) 20 universités bénéficiaires sont sélectionnées sur appel à candidatures(EN)

(3.2) 20 centres universitaires d’employabilité sont accompagnés et mis en capacité(EN)

(3.3) 20 centres universitaires d’employabilité sont équipés(EN)

(3.4) Les besoins en compétences professionnelles, transversales et numériques sont anticipés(EN)

(3.5) Les 20 centres sont mis en réseau(EN)

(4.1) Des sessions de formations de formateurs en compétences transversales sont organisées(EN)

(4.2) Des sessions de formations de formateurs en compétences numériques sont organisées(EN)

(4.3) Des micro-certifications sont proposées(EN)

(5.1) Les centres sont mis en capacité de proposer des formations professionnalisantes en numérique(EN)

(5.2) Des sessions de formation professionnelle de publics vulnérables sont proposées et accompagnées(EN)

(5.3) Une plateforme d’offres d’emploi est mise en place (Chypre, Égypte, Jordanie, Liban, Palestine)(EN)

(6.1) Un statut national d’étudiant-entrepreneur est proposé à Chypre, en Égypte, Jordanie et Palestine(EN)

(6.2) Un pôle local d’entrepreneuriat étudiant est mis en place par chaque centre(EN)

(6.3) Des formations de formateurs à l’accompagnement des étudiants entrepreneurs sont organisées(EN)

(6.4) 20 pré-incubateurs universitaires sont mis en place ou renforcés(EN)

project_summary:
PROJECT TYPE: 1 - Thematic projects
~
MAIN INTERVENTION FIELD: 140. Support for labour market matching and transitions
~
SUMMARY: Le projet EUSEEDS (Enable, Upgrade and Spread Employability, Entrepreneurship and Digital Skills) vise à déployer 20 centres universitaires dans 5 pays est-méditerranéens pour développer l’employabilité et les compétences numériques de la jeunesse.
Les pays méditerranéens concernés partagent nombre de défis communs telles qu’une jeunesse éprouvant des difficultés à s’insérer sur le marché de l’emploi formel. Cet enjeu de stabilité est imputable à une massification de l’accès à l’enseignement supérieur couplée à une inadéquation entre formations universitaires et besoins en main d’œuvre, qui crée un déficit généralisé d’employabilité. Le potentiel de création de richesse par le numérique reste trop réduit.
Proposé par un consortium équilibré et solide réunissant des partenaires de référence (Chypre, Égypte, France, Jordanie, Liban, Palestine), le projet, qui vise un impact massif en termes de personnes formées (7100 bénéficiaires finaux dans le cadre du projet, 4100 ensuite en flux annuel, objectifs atteignables), prévoit une réponse collective à des défis communs, sur la base d’initiatives déjà éprouvées, mises à l’échelle, pour une meilleure insertion professionnelle, en améliorant l’employabilité d’étudiants et jeunes diplômés, en développant leurs compétences professionnelles en numérique, leurs compétences transversales et l’auto-entrepreneuriat.
Les étudiants et jeunes diplômés ne seront pas les seuls bénéficiaires finaux. Les centres établis cibleront des publics spécifiques vulnérables au sein de leur communauté comme des jeunes sans emploi, en proposant des formations adaptées et à distance.
Le modèle consiste à équiper et doter des compétences nécessaires 20 centres de ressources universitaires pérennes situés dans des zones à fort enjeu social des 5 pays, dont la mission sera de fournir durablement des services internes (formations, préincubation de projets) et externes vers la communauté (formations à la littéracie et à la professionnalisation numérique).
~

project_name: MEDiterranean Youth for Creative Impact
programme: 2021 - 2027 Interreg VI-B NEXT Mediterranean Sea Basin (NEXT MED)
topic_keywords: training | learning | education | e-learning | skills | methodology | methodologies | handbook | communication | entrepreneur | entrepreneurs | platform | youth | women
project_summary:
PROJECT TYPE: 2 - Youth-oriented projects
~
MAIN INTERVENTION FIELD: 152. Measures to promote equal opportunities and active participation in society
~
SUMMARY: The MED-Y.C.I project addresses transnational challenges in the Mediterranean region, targeting educational disparities, digital gap, and high youth unemployment. With a significant youth demographic facing low civic engagement, and informal employment, particularly among young women, the project unites France, Spain, Turkey, Tunisia, and Egypt to co-develop an innovative training program for youth Civil Society Organizations (CSOs).
MED-Y.C.I aims to foster digital, creative, and analytical skills crucial for new job markets and active civic participation. By pooling expertise from multidisciplinary experts and artists across five countries, the project develops new educational practices that promote lifelong learning and regional solidarity. The initiative integrates international exchanges, cross-border and regional actions led by youth, rethinking engagement as a cross-cutting skill for better employability and empowerment.

In this frame, MED-Y-CI aims to train 200 youths aged 18-30 from diverse backgrounds through an original joint scheme training within an analytical, a creative and a practical part. Additionally, video facilities, known as MED-Y.C.I Studios, are established in each partner country to serve as hubs for ongoing skill development and innovation. Managed by the youth, they impact at least 500 additional youngsters.

Thanks to this infrastructure, transnational WebTV shows (Medtlaks) and creative engaged events are produced by trained youngsters and shared in international events, to advocate with regional authorities. Moreover, the project plan to involve 50 young entrepreneurs showcasing their initiatives for positive changes, highlighting different mediterranean approaches. In addition, to ensure the replicability and sustainability of the project’s methodologies, a MED-Y.C.I Handbook and E-learning Platform are created.
~

partner_name: University of Galway (Office of the Vice-President for Research & Innovation)

project_summary:
A pioneering initiative set to transform the cosmeceutical and nutraceutical industries by utilizing bioactives and biopolymers from microalgae. This project introduces innovative, low-impact methods to produce high-value ingredients, replacing synthetic additives while reducing water use and minimizing waste.
By optimizing microalgae cultivation and recovering valuable by-products, the project demonstrates scalable solutions that support a shift toward renewable, eco-friendly alternatives. Key outputs include pilot actions, innovative biotechnological solutions, and high-value bio-based products for industrial adoption, benefiting SMEs, researchers, and blue biotechnology industries.
With a transnational approach, the initiative integrates regional expertise, fosters knowledge transfer, and drives sustainable growth in the Atlantic’s blue economy. By accelerating innovation and competitiveness in the bioeconomy sector,it paves the way for a more sustainable future.

project_name: Transforming Rivers by Reducing Aquatic Plastic Pollution
partner_name: Associação do Instituto Superior Técnico para a Investigação e Desenvolvimento (Civil Engineering Research and Innovation for Sustainability)
project_summary:
TRAP addresses the critical transnational challenge of aquatic plastic pollution through innovative, eco-friendly solutions to reduce plastic litter reaching the Atlantic Ocean. The main goal is to produce guidelines to design hotspots to trap and retain plastics in rivers and estuaries. It offers an innovative approach to tackle aquatic plastic pollution by combining advanced digital tools, nature-based solutions and community engagement.
TRAP brings positive change in the efficiency of plastic removal strategies and better-informed management practices.
Key outputs include a digital management tool for identifying plastic retention hotspots, field-tested retention solutions, and practical guidelines for decision-makers. Beneficiaries range from environmental agencies to local authorities and riverine communities.
The project leverages transnational cooperation to ensure knowledge sharing, creating scalable, field-tested solutions that can be adapted across the Atlantic Area.

project_name: Mediterranean initiative to REgenerate wastewater Sludges for Optimal Use towaRd a Circular Economy
programme: 2021 - 2027 Interreg VI-B NEXT Mediterranean Sea Basin (NEXT MED)
topic_keywords: capacity building | capacity | toolkit | guidelines | communication | reuse | beneficiaries | communities
project_summary:
PROJECT TYPE: 1 - Thematic projects
~
MAIN INTERVENTION FIELD:

066. Waste water collection and treatment compliant with energy efficiency criteria
~
SUMMARY: MedRESOURCE tackles the pressing transnational challenges of water scarcity, land degradation, and waste management in the Mediterranean region. Currently, despite existing standards, WW sludge is still inadequately managed, leading to environmental harm and missed opportunities for resource recovery. MedRESOURCE will develop and demonstrate four innovative solutions to reuse wastewater sludge to enhance soil fertility, reduce environmental pollution, and foster sustainable agricultural practices.
The solutions will be jointly developed by a partnership of 6 organizations from 4 countries: Palestine, Italy, Tunisia and Jordan, supported by institutional and local associated partners. In each country a Living Lab will be set up to further develop existing solutions and to demonstrate their value to local communities, farmers, authorities and policy makers.
The expected change from MedRESOURCE is profound: converting waste into valuable resources, the project will significantly improve soil quality, reduce the need for chemical fertilizers, and mitigate pollution. This will enhance agricultural productivity and resilience to climate change, benefiting local farmers, communities, and the environment.
Key outputs are:
-	4 Living Labs (LL), each demonstrating a specific reuse of WW sludge in agriculture
-	A capacity building programme carried out in each LL, reaching about 200 beneficiaries
-	4 international study visist to the LL, promoting replicability and best practice exchange and reaching from 60 to 120 beneficiaries
-	Guidelines for policies development, providing references and standards at EU and MED level to integrate and better adapt regulatory frameworks to the innovative potential of WW sludge reuse.
MedRESOURCE offers a compelling solution to critical environmental issues, providing tangible benefits and fostering sustainable development across the Mediterranean. Supporting this project means investing in a greener, more resilient future for the region.
~

project_name: Resilience ENhancing to adapt FORests to Climate change Effects and to prevent biotic and abiotic hazard
project_summary:
In Mediterranean regions hit by wildfires and windstorms, RENFORCE shifts forest management from reaction to prevention. In other words, the project develops practical tools to cut risks before disaster strikes.
It sets up participatory prevention plans in pilot zones, combining local knowledge with forestry science. On the ground, this means mapping high-risk areas, managing vegetation to act as natural firebreaks, and applying storm-resistant planting strategies.
RENFORCE also builds common protocols across borders to anticipate damage. It delivers decision-making tools for public authorities, training programmes for landowners, and a shared strategy to embed risk reduction into forest planning.

project_name: Eco-nautical sea routes
project_summary:
As the world's largest tourist destination, Mediterranean ports are under intense tourist pressure. This makes their protected areas more vulnerable to overtourism.
The ECO-SEAROUTES project aims to create an eco-nautical route linking ports committed to: 1) respect a common sustainability standard in a transnational approach; 2) redistribute tourism flows to neighbouring coastal and inland areas.
To achieve this, the project encourages local decision-makers to create and develop new local tourist routes to less crowded areas and their cultural heritage. It will use a bottom-up and participatory approach, ready to be shared and replicated.

project_name: Eco-nautical sea routes
partner_name: Κυπριακό Ινστιτούτο για τη Θάλασσα και τη Ναυτιλία
project_summary:
As the world's largest tourist destination, Mediterranean ports are under intense tourist pressure. This makes their protected areas more vulnerable to overtourism.
The ECO-SEAROUTES project aims to create an eco-nautical route linking ports committed to: 1) respect a common sustainability standard in a transnational approach; 2) redistribute tourism flows to neighbouring coastal and inland areas.
To achieve this, the project encourages local decision-makers to create and develop new local tourist routes to less crowded areas and their cultural heritage. It will use a bottom-up and participatory approach, ready to be shared and replicated.

project_name: Inclusive Coastal Resilience in the Euro-Med
topic_keywords: education | course | workshop | methodology | methodologies | dissemination | communication | sme
project_summary:
INCORE-MED brings citizens back into the heart of coastal planning. In Italy, Spain, Portugal, and Croatia, the project tests two advanced participation tools to improve coastal and marine policies: SWAP and Democracy Labs.
The SWAP tool (Scenario Workshops + Adaptation Pathways) helps local actors co-design realistic climate adaptation plans by blending community visions with expert climate projections, using scenario mapping and multicriteria analysis. As for Democracy Labs, it creates discussion spaces where citizens and authorities co-develop new rules and formats for public engagement in marine planning.
These tools are deployed to: 1) create and update participatory cartographies and climate risk perception maps; 2) integrate nature-based solutions in local action plans; 3) disseminate the methodology to new territories through methodological insights and online courses.
specific_objective: RSO2.4. Promoting climate change adaptation and disaster risk prevention and resilience, taking into account eco-system based approaches

project_name: Green and circular transformation of services in Med tourism
partner_name: STEP RI znanstveno-tehnologijski park Sveučilišta u Rijeci d.o.o.
topic_keywords: course | competence | capabilities | methodology | handbook | business support
project_summary:
The Mediterranean tourism sector is largely made up of small and family businesses. That is why GreenSET develops a business support infrastructure to encourage as many of them as possible to innovate services they provide to reduce their environmental impact and better use resources.
Each business will create new competitive and sustainable service, enabling new revenues from existing resources and capabilities, supported by local MED experts, trained and certified in service innovation methodology. In this way, GreenSET aims for the long-term adoption of sustainable practices by a wide range of businesses, with each successful experience having the potential to spread to other ones!

project_name: Monitoring cumulative Impact to guide mitigation and RestorAtion in the MediterrAnean Region
topic_keywords: training | education | training materials | capacity | methodology | toolkit | toolkits | stakeholder engagement | presenting | sme
project_summary: Humans are placing increasing strain on ecosystems exposing them to multiple, concurrent stressors. The Mediterranean region is a biodiversity hotspot hosting almost half of all the plant species, about a third of all the vertebrate species on our planet and an exceptional number of species found nowhere else in the world. This biodiversity, however, is heavily exposed to stressors like legacy and emerging contaminants, marine litter including microplastics, noise, habitat destruction from ever-increasing human activities, exacerbated by climate change, resulting in long-term impacts on ecosystem stability. These stressors are often tackled in isolation. The urgency to address these threats and the cumulative impact of multiple stressors requires cohesive, transnational efforts that combine fragmented knowledge to develop a new, integrated approach. In response to these challenges, the MIRAMAR project has identified three ecologically significant ecosystems in the Euro-Mediterranean region: seagrass meadows, wetlands, and habitats of endangered species. Seagrass meadows serve as crucial breeding, feeding, and sheltering grounds for numerous species, are vital for oxygen production and as blue carbon sinks. Wetlands are biodiverse transition ecosystems containing river deltas, brackish waters, salt lakes, and marshes, supporting a variety of flora and fauna, that play a critical role in maintaining ecological balance. Healthy endangered species habitats, including those of seabirds, cetaceans and wetland birds, are vital for the survival of these vulnerable populations. MIRAMAR’s 8 partners represent 6 countries across the Euro-Med region (Albania, Croatia, France, Greece, Italy, and Spain), and come from academia (UNISI), research institutes (CSIC, HCMR, SZN, RBI), NGO networks (MIO-ECSDE), as well as regional (OEC) and national authorities (MTE).Thanks to this transnational collaboration, MIRAMAR aims to develop an innovative harmonized methodology to monitor cumulative impact of selected stressors in 9 pilot areas in the three identified habitats. Drawing support and upscaling previous project results (INTERREG, ADRION, LIFE, H2020) MIRAMAR will design, test, and validate a new holistic monitoring method to identify suitable nature-based mitigation and restoration solutions. By aligning with national monitoring strategies and the management plans of parks and protected areas, MIRAMAR will address the common challenge of preventing, mitigating and restoring environmental degradation of these ecosystems. MIRAMAR is committed to aligning with key environmental initiatives and programs such as, Zero Pollution Action Plan of the European Green Deal, EU Biodiversity Strategy for 2030, EU Marine Strategy Framework Directive, Barcelona Convention's Integrated Monitoring and Assessment Programme, Union for the Mediterranean, UN Environment Programme/Mediterranean Action Plan, Ramsar Convention on Wetlands, among others. These frameworks emphasize the interconnected nature of climate change, biodiversity loss, land and sea degradation, stressing the importance of addressing these challenges in a coherent, synergetic, and holistic manner based on best available scientific knowledge.By engaging with these international agreements and frameworks, MIRAMAR will contribute to global efforts to tackle environmental issues and promote sustainable development practices. MIRAMAR’s collaborative approach and focus on stakeholder engagement, benefitting from Living Labs, underscores our commitment to harnessing collective expertise and fostering innovative solutions to address the complex challenges facing the Euro-Mediterranean region. Fourteen associate partners, representing key stakeholders in each targeted pilot area, will be proactively engaged in the project. They will see their scientific knowledge and capacity to implement environmental management practices and policies increase; more importantly, they will benefit from the project outcomes through the monitoring protocols and operational roadmap. This process of engagement will take concrete form with the signing of Declaration of Intent to adopt MIRAMARs harmonised monitoring protocol to assess cumulative impacts, as a key commitment to identify the "best" mitigation and restoration solutions. Through partnerships with diverse stakeholders, alignment with international initiatives, and a focus on science-based solutions, MIRAMAR is positioned to make a meaningful impact to protect nature, biodiversity, and green infrastructure for the benefit of current and future generations. With a comprehensive and inclusive approach, MIRAMAR aims to lead the way in addressing complex environmental challenges and charting a resilient, biodiverse, and sustainable future for all.

Fourteen associate partners, representing key stakeholders in each targeted pilot area, will be proactively engaged in the project. They will see their scientific knowledge and capacity to implement environmental management practices and policies increase; more importantly, they will benefit from the project outcomes through the monitoring protocols and operational roadmap. This process of engagement will take concrete form with the signing of Declaration of Intent to adopt MIRAMARs harmonised monitoring protocol to assess cumulative impacts, as a key commitment to identify the "best" mitigation and restoration solutions. Through partnerships with diverse stakeholders, alignment with international initiatives, and a focus on science-based solutions, MIRAMAR is positioned to make a meaningful impact to protect nature, biodiversity, and green infrastructure for the benefit of current and future generations. With a comprehensive and inclusive approach, MIRAMAR aims to lead the way in addressing complex environmental challenges and charting a resilient, biodiverse, and sustainable future for all.

project_name: Monitoring cumulative Impact to guide mitigation and RestorAtion in the MediterrAnean Region
topic_keywords: training | education | training materials | capacity | methodology | toolkit | toolkits | stakeholder engagement | presenting | sme
project_summary: Humans are placing increasing strain on ecosystems exposing them to multiple, concurrent stressors. The Mediterranean region is a biodiversity hotspot hosting almost half of all the plant species, about a third of all the vertebrate species on our planet and an exceptional number of species found nowhere else in the world. This biodiversity, however, is heavily exposed to stressors like legacy and emerging contaminants, marine litter including microplastics, noise, habitat destruction from ever-increasing human activities, exacerbated by climate change, resulting in long-term impacts on ecosystem stability. These stressors are often tackled in isolation. The urgency to address these threats and the cumulative impact of multiple stressors requires cohesive, transnational efforts that combine fragmented knowledge to develop a new, integrated approach. In response to these challenges, the MIRAMAR project has identified three ecologically significant ecosystems in the Euro-Mediterranean region: seagrass meadows, wetlands, and habitats of endangered species. Seagrass meadows serve as crucial breeding, feeding, and sheltering grounds for numerous species, are vital for oxygen production and as blue carbon sinks. Wetlands are biodiverse transition ecosystems containing river deltas, brackish waters, salt lakes, and marshes, supporting a variety of flora and fauna, that play a critical role in maintaining ecological balance. Healthy endangered species habitats, including those of seabirds, cetaceans and wetland birds, are vital for the survival of these vulnerable populations. MIRAMAR’s 8 partners represent 6 countries across the Euro-Med region (Albania, Croatia, France, Greece, Italy, and Spain), and come from academia (UNISI), research institutes (CSIC, HCMR, SZN, RBI), NGO networks (MIO-ECSDE), as well as regional (OEC) and national authorities (MTE).Thanks to this transnational collaboration, MIRAMAR aims to develop an innovative harmonized methodology to monitor cumulative impact of selected stressors in 9 pilot areas in the three identified habitats. Drawing support and upscaling previous project results (INTERREG, ADRION, LIFE, H2020) MIRAMAR will design, test, and validate a new holistic monitoring method to identify suitable nature-based mitigation and restoration solutions. By aligning with national monitoring strategies and the management plans of parks and protected areas, MIRAMAR will address the common challenge of preventing, mitigating and restoring environmental degradation of these ecosystems. MIRAMAR is committed to aligning with key environmental initiatives and programs such as, Zero Pollution Action Plan of the European Green Deal, EU Biodiversity Strategy for 2030, EU Marine Strategy Framework Directive, Barcelona Convention's Integrated Monitoring and Assessment Programme, Union for the Mediterranean, UN Environment Programme/Mediterranean Action Plan, Ramsar Convention on Wetlands, among others. These frameworks emphasize the interconnected nature of climate change, biodiversity loss, land and sea degradation, stressing the importance of addressing these challenges in a coherent, synergetic, and holistic manner based on best available scientific knowledge.By engaging with these international agreements and frameworks, MIRAMAR will contribute to global efforts to tackle environmental issues and promote sustainable development practices. MIRAMAR’s collaborative approach and focus on stakeholder engagement, benefitting from Living Labs, underscores our commitment to harnessing collective expertise and fostering innovative solutions to address the complex challenges facing the Euro-Mediterranean region. Fourteen associate partners, representing key stakeholders in each targeted pilot area, will be proactively engaged in the project. They will see their scientific knowledge and capacity to implement environmental management practices and policies increase; more importantly, they will benefit from the project outcomes through the monitoring protocols and operational roadmap. This process of engagement will take concrete form with the signing of Declaration of Intent to adopt MIRAMARs harmonised monitoring protocol to assess cumulative impacts, as a key commitment to identify the "best" mitigation and restoration solutions. Through partnerships with diverse stakeholders, alignment with international initiatives, and a focus on science-based solutions, MIRAMAR is positioned to make a meaningful impact to protect nature, biodiversity, and green infrastructure for the benefit of current and future generations. With a comprehensive and inclusive approach, MIRAMAR aims to lead the way in addressing complex environmental challenges and charting a resilient, biodiverse, and sustainable future for all.

project_name: Port-City Regions To Wave Med Cruise Destinations As Smart, Sustainable And Striking Eco-Tourism Systems
partner_name: Chambre de Commerce et d'Industrie du Var
topic_keywords: workshop | pilot action | pilot actions | business support | platform | online platform
project_summary:
Cruise tourists are increasingly seeking tailor-made inland tours. They want to discover more authentic places in a sustainable way, in tours that suit their curiosity as well as their physical abilities.
The eco-tourism platform PortCREW will be tested in 5 Mediterranean cruise ports located in Spain, France, Italy, Slovenia, and Greece. What is it about? Providing cruise managers, port or tourism authorities with new inland tourism packages. How will it help tourism management? By avoiding overtourism in the same seaside areas, valorising new natural and cultural assets and increasing the social acceptance of the cruise sector in MED cities.

project_name: Sustainable Microalgae and Cyanobacteria-Based Bioactive Compounds for Cosmeceuticals and Nutraceuticals
topic_keywords: knowledge transfer | pilot action | pilot actions | uptake | scalable | sme | smes
project_summary:
A pioneering initiative set to transform the cosmeceutical and nutraceutical industries by utilizing bioactives and biopolymers from microalgae. This project introduces innovative, low-impact methods to produce high-value ingredients, replacing synthetic additives while reducing water use and minimizing waste.
By optimizing microalgae cultivation and recovering valuable by-products, the project demonstrates scalable solutions that support a shift toward renewable, eco-friendly alternatives. Key outputs include pilot actions, innovative biotechnological solutions, and high-value bio-based products for industrial adoption, benefiting SMEs, researchers, and blue biotechnology industries.
With a transnational approach, the initiative integrates regional expertise, fosters knowledge transfer, and drives sustainable growth in the Atlantic’s blue economy. By accelerating innovation and competitiveness in the bioeconomy sector,it paves the way for a more sustainable future.

programme: 2021 - 2027 Interreg VI-B NEXT Mediterranean Sea Basin (NEXT MED)

project_summary:
PROJECT TYPE: 2 - Youth-oriented projects
~
MAIN INTERVENTION FIELD:

151. Support for adult education (excluding infrastructure)
~
SUMMARY: MEDSHIFTGREEN directly addresses the pressing transnational challenges of environmental degradation and resource depletion in the Mediterranean by revolutionizing education through the integration of nature-based solutions and green technologies into traditional curricula. This initiative seeks to transform the educational landscape, fostering a generation of youth who are well-equipped to tackle and mitigate ecological issues across borders. MEDSHIFTGREEN approach shifts from conventional learning paradigms to a holistic, sustainability-focused education model that provides young people with the critical skills necessary to innovate within the green economy. The expected change is profound as we aim to fill the current gap in specialized training in sustainable practices and increase environmental awareness among regional youth. Key outputs of the project include the establishment of Interactive Green Labs across Greek, Lebanese, Turkish & Cyprus educational institutions and beyond, integration of sustainability into academic programs via the NBS Curriculum, and the implementation of Green Skills Bootcamps that offer practical, economy-driven environmental education. These elements are designed to empower students with new competencies, provide educators with necessary resources and methodologies for teaching sustainability, and ultimately benefit local communities and industries through innovative, applicable environmental solutions developed by students. MEDSHIFTGREEN not only promises significant educational evolution but also ensures that Mediterranean youth are prepared to lead sustainable & inclusive growth in their communities, turning ecological challenges into opportunities for innovation and collaboration. This project will cultivate a generation that thrives on solving problems together, enhancing both their employability and their proactive engagement in preserving our planet.
~

project_name: Developing Inclusive Education in the Mediterranean
programme: 2021 - 2027 Interreg VI-B NEXT Mediterranean Sea Basin (NEXT MED)
topic_keywords: training | trainings | learning | education | course | skills | capacity building | capacity development | capacity | knowledge transfer | toolkit | dissemination | communication | replication | scalability | beneficiaries | youth | women | communities
project_summary:
PROJECT TYPE: 1 - Thematic projects
~
MAIN INTERVENTION FIELD:

151. Support for adult education (excluding infrastructure)
~
SUMMARY: The Mediterranean is united by some major challenges related to the development of its different areas and communities, especially regarding the gap between cultural heritage developmental potential and the social and economic precariousness prevailing around important cultural sites.
Education, continuous training and skills development represent a key tool both for strengthening the potential of territories and for promoting inclusion, empowerment and social cohesion.
Furthermore, Cultural Heritage (CH) is one of the most sound, common and recognizable Mediterranean traits as well as an acknowledged driver for its development.
Accordingly, its main objective will be reinforcing and innovating the consortium's different learning/teaching systems to make them more integrated and compatible. Besides, the focus on Cultural Heritage will allow DIEM to exploit the acknowledged multiplier effect of CH-oriented activities in terms of know-how exchanging, empowerment of people and, eventually, economic growth.
Hence, DIEM is expected to contribute to social inclusion and to skills development for the unintegrated groups (youth, women, rural inhabitants and NEETs). Capitalizing on the experience of its partners, DIEM will move along 3 concurrent lines of development: deliver a cascade model of teaching/training programs for 3 groups (A-Leaders, B-Future Managers, C-Local communities); setup a toolkit for teaching/learning capable to combine knowledge, emerging technologies (i.e. AI) and the lessons learned from the pandemic; assume DIEM as an occasion for the first beneficiaries and for broader communities, the assets and territories belonging to the 6 countries of DIEM consortium (IT, EGY, JOR, TUN, ES,PS).
The project entails 3 technical WPs: Standardization (Partners design systems for international cooperation), Training around pilot areas (TOT courses and inclusion-oriented trainings), Scalability (replication on youth and extension to broader communities).
~
KEEP KEYWORDS: Cultural heritage and arts, Education and training, Social inclusion and equal opportunities

project_summary:
PROJECT TYPE: 1 - Thematic projects
~
MAIN INTERVENTION FIELD:

151. Support for adult education (excluding infrastructure)
~
SUMMARY: The Mediterranean is united by some major challenges related to the development of its different areas and communities, especially regarding the gap between cultural heritage developmental potential and the social and economic precariousness prevailing around important cultural sites.
Education, continuous training and skills development represent a key tool both for strengthening the potential of territories and for promoting inclusion, empowerment and social cohesion.
Furthermore, Cultural Heritage (CH) is one of the most sound, common and recognizable Mediterranean traits as well as an acknowledged driver for its development.
Accordingly, its main objective will be reinforcing and innovating the consortium's different learning/teaching systems to make them more integrated and compatible. Besides, the focus on Cultural Heritage will allow DIEM to exploit the acknowledged multiplier effect of CH-oriented activities in terms of know-how exchanging, empowerment of people and, eventually, economic growth.
Hence, DIEM is expected to contribute to social inclusion and to skills development for the unintegrated groups (youth, women, rural inhabitants and NEETs). Capitalizing on the experience of its partners, DIEM will move along 3 concurrent lines of development: deliver a cascade model of teaching/training programs for 3 groups (A-Leaders, B-Future Managers, C-Local communities); setup a toolkit for teaching/learning capable to combine knowledge, emerging technologies (i.e. AI) and the lessons learned from the pandemic; assume DIEM as an occasion for the first beneficiaries and for broader communities, the assets and territories belonging to the 6 countries of DIEM consortium (IT, EGY, JOR, TUN, ES,PS).
The project entails 3 technical WPs: Standardization (Partners design systems for international cooperation), Training around pilot areas (TOT courses and inclusion-oriented trainings), Scalability (replication on youth and extension to broader communities).
~
KEEP KEYWORDS: Cultural heritage and arts, Education and training, Social inclusion and equal opportunities

project_summary:
PROJECT TYPE: 2 - Youth-oriented projects
~
MAIN INTERVENTION FIELD:
~
SUMMARY: The educational landscape lacks comprehensive coverage of the socio-political, economic & technical dimensions of the ecosystem, hindering the root causes & ramifications of the eco-crisis. Our project aims to develop a holistic environmental training program for higher education students & an advocacy initiative through network building. We strive to transform individuals' perceptions of social & environmental ecosystems & confront nature preservation challenges through interdisciplinary training driven by the collaborative efforts of academics, researchers, & experts from diverse disciplines.
The environmental crisis is complex & part of a global polycrisis. Despite local variations, its systematic nature shares common traits. National actors often focus on local symptoms without addressing underlying causes. The systematic structure of the environmental crisis calls for collective solutions based on contemporary approaches & evidence-based knowledge.
Within this scope our goal is to
-Strengthen interdisciplinary collaboration to develop & promote consensus-based approaches & solutions for green transition
-Foster knowledge sharing through training programs highlighting the interconnected nature of environmental & social ecosystems
-Enhance blue & green upskilling among higher education students
-Build capacity for long-term network & advocacy

By enhancing blue & green upskilling, we anticipate a significant shift in how young adults perceive & address environmental challenges. The training programs will nurture solution-oriented mindsets among university students entering the workforce. By fostering interdisciplinary collaboration & empowering young adults with skills, we envision a generation equipped to tackle environmental issues in their everyday lives. Beneficiaries extend beyond higher education students to society as a whole. In the long term, we aim to create a ripple effect impacting sectors, & actors, with broader adult education through our network.
~

project_name: Fostering Transnational Knowledge for More Inclusive and Participatory Mediterranean
programme: 2021 - 2027 Interreg VI-B NEXT Mediterranean Sea Basin (NEXT MED)
topic_keywords: learning | capacity | technology transfer | handbook | beneficiaries | communities
project_summary:
PROJECT TYPE: 3 - Governance Projects
~
MAIN INTERVENTION FIELD:

173. Enhancing institutional capacity of public authorities and stakeholders to implement territorial cooperation projects and initiatives in a cross-border, transnational, maritime and inter-regional context
~
SUMMARY: The lack of participatory planning for social inclusion programs on the part of local authorities has led to a misguided conception in identifying real needs and inclusive understanding of the pressing social challenges across the Mediterranean. This has hindered the development of society driven solutions that effectively respond to these challenges. TRANSFORMED will foster cross-sector synergies through a transnational cooperation to jointly develop solutions to facilitate the establishment of efficient governance models for improving social inclusion at local level.
The project proposal comprises analysing, complementing and disseminating the transnational knowledge demonstrated in implementing 4 regionally-acclaimed governance models that have adopted bottom-up and participatory approaches to deliver inclusive community-serving programs; the objective being to exploit and upscale this knowledge.
The outputs are complementary, using a building blocks approach. They are:
-Transnational Handbook, that is a fruit of 5 transnational Community-engagement symposia, Online data analysis clusters and a practitioners’ lab;
-Enhanced institutional capacities of 5 local authorities and 5 social inclusion civic actors, thanks to the project’s call for inclusive Local Planning concept notes, the Peer-to-peer knowledge capsules and the procedure demonstration pilots;
- 5 Blueprints with jointly developed social inclusion community-serving programs operated by tandems between local authorities and civil society actors responding to inclusion challenges identified on the national level. Inclusive Local Planning Blueprints resulting from Peer-to-peer mentoring, enhanced by national round table debates and will be introduced in a regional knowledge sharing exhibition.
Final beneficiaries are local authorities and civic actors from partner countries benefiting from best practices of participatory governance, and local communities benefiting from the improved local governance.
~
KEEP KEYWORDS: Community integration and common identity, Governance, partnership, Knowledge and technology transfer
specific_objective: ISO6.6. Other actions to support better cooperation governance (all strands)
outputs:
(1.1) Project management meetings(EN)

(1.2) Monitoring, Evaluation, Accountability and Learning - MEAL System(EN)

(1.3) Follow up and consolidation reports(EN)

(2.1) TRANSFORMED info kit - visibility and outreach(EN)

(2.2) Networking and capitalisation(EN)

(3.1) Community-based data analysis Handbook(EN)

(4.1) Improved institutional capacity of local authorities and civic actors working in social inclusion(EN)

(5.1) Jointly developed Blueprints for social inclusion community-serving programs(EN)

topic_keywords: capacity building | capacity | capability | capabilities | technology transfer | dissemination | awareness | awareness raising | communication | platform
project_summary:
PROJECT TYPE: 1 - Thematic projects
~
MAIN INTERVENTION FIELD: 060. Adaptation to climate change measures and prevention and management of climate related risks: others, e.g. storms and drought (including awareness raising, civil protection and disaster management systems, infrastructures and ecosystem based approaches)
~
SUMMARY: The Mediterranean Sea is particularly sensitive to climate change, warming 20% faster than the global average while already under significant anthropogenic pressures like shipping, fishing, and tourism. Issues such as rising sea water temperatures, increasing salinity and invasive species are already negatively impacting marine ecosystems and affecting fisheries and tourism. The eastern Mediterranean in particular is a hotspot of rising sea water temperatures and at the same time a major gap in terms of marine observations. Countries in this region are in urgent need of increased climate resilience and adaptation capacity. It is expected that the frequency and intensity of extreme events will increase. Such events include storm surges, erosion, flash floods, marine heat waves (MHW) and Mediterranean hurricanes (“Medicanes”). Further, coastal areas are susceptible to maritime safety and marine pollution incidents because of a lack of operational capacity and situational awareness of the marine environment (despite the high volume of ship traffic and coastal economic activity).
To respond to scientific and societal challenges in the area, there is the need for a sustainable and cost-effective multiplatform observing system. We propose the combined use of low-maintenance, user-friendly multiplatform observing systems for a more comprehensive 3D characterization of the region at multiple scales: High-Frequency Radars (HFRs), Ferryboxes, coastal fixed point observatories and in-situ sensors. The need for setting up cost-effective observing systems in the area is further justified by the clear north-south and west-east imbalance of monitoring capabilities. Setting up a multi-level, multi-sector, and cross-border approach in adapting to climate change is an urgent need, and it expected to enhance the capability of eastern Mediterranean countries to jointly develop and and enhance innovation and actions for climate change adaptation, risk prevention & disaster resilience.
~

project_name: Nature based solutions for climate resilient Mediterranean cities
programme: 2021 - 2027 Interreg VI-B NEXT Mediterranean Sea Basin (NEXT MED)
topic_keywords: toolkit | pilot action | awareness | awareness raising | communication | uptake | scalable | platform | online platform | beneficiaries
project_summary:
PROJECT TYPE: 1 - Thematic projects
~
MAIN INTERVENTION FIELD: 060. Adaptation to climate change measures and prevention and management of climate related risks: others, e.g. storms and drought (including awareness raising, civil protection and disaster management systems, infrastructures and ecosystem based approaches)
~
SUMMARY: CHALLENGE ADDRESSED
Extreme weather events represent one of the primary climate risks in the Mediterranean, with urban areas, where the majority of the population resides, being exceptionally vulnerable due to a combination of densely-built spaces, impervious surfaces, and high population density. Furthermore, the growing intensity and frequency of these events (a direct result of climate change) makes conventional infrastructure (such as drainage systems), on which current climate resilience approaches are largely dependent, increasingly inadequate. To address this common challenge, nature-based solutions represent a scalable, cost-effective and environmentally sustainable approach with increasing popularity worldwide, yet with limited applications in the Mediterranean.

PROJECT IMPACT
NBS4MED aspires to transform urban climate adaptation strategies in the Mediterranean by advancing the adoption of nature-based solutions and their broad integration in urban climate resilience planning. To this end, the project will:
-Strengthen organisational capacities to design and implement fit-for-purpose nature-based solutions.
-Stimulate improvements in urban climate adaptation policies in project territories.
-Enhance transnational cooperation and exchange of knowledge.

KEY OUTPUTS
-A common decision making framework to guide the operationalisation of nature-based solutions across the Mediterranean.
-5 replicable nature-based solutions implemented and tested in project territories.
-Local strategies to accelerate the uptake of nature-based solutions in 5 target municipalities.
-A transnational network to enhance cooperation in urban climate adaptation and advance the deployment of nature-based solutions across the Mediterranean.

FINAL BENEFICIARIES
Urban populations in project territories will increase their climate-resilience and benefit from improved quality of life as a result of the increased deployment of nature-based solutions.
~

project_summary:
PROJECT TYPE: 1 - Thematic projects
~
MAIN INTERVENTION FIELD: 059. Adaptation to climate change measures and prevention and management of climate related risks: fires (including awareness raising, civil protection and disaster management systems, infrastructures and ecosystem-based approaches)
~
SUMMARY: Wildfires are worsening in the Mediterranean due to longer fire seasons, and mega-fires. A key challenge is ensuring wildfire management uses science-based innovations. SIMMER invites scientists, practitioners, and decision-makers to collaborate, share best practices to improve management. This includes understanding wildfire behavior, trends, and climate change impacts to better manage landscapes, ecosystems, and response effectiveness.
SIMMER uniquely analyzes the interplay between scientific, legal, and operationalization context to develop the cornerstone Integrated Science-Policy Framework for sustainable wildfire management. It bridges the gap between advanced technological tools and their practical application, enhancing stakeholders' management capacity across the entire lifecycle—from preparedness to response, and recovery.
SIMMER holistic approach starts by collecting stakeholder needs, mapping existing wildfire management assets, developing maturity indicators, and identifying gaps. Next, SIMMER creates the integrated science-policy framework to address these gaps, which is tested through pilot activities. In the final phase, SIMMER produces a Knowledge Hub and Networking Platform, drafting policy recommendations, and training materials based on lessons learned.
SIMMER pilot activities in six countries involve civil protection, nature protection organizations, local authorities, and volunteers. Demonstrable tools include ecosystem-based methods like prescribed burning and grazing, AI, remote sensing, for risk assessment and early warning, drones for burn severity mapping, and community-based post-fire assessment.
SIMMER offers stakeholders mature scientific solutions, legal frameworks and operationalization protocols, benefiting decision-makers, firefighters, community leaders, and researchers. Beyond providing tools, SIMMER fosters collaboration and knowledge sharing, empowering communities to proactively protect themselves and their environment.
~
