project_name: Just and Green Transition through Participatory Governance
programme: 2021 - 2027 Interreg VI-B NEXT Mediterranean Sea Basin (NEXT MED)
topic_keywords: capacity building | capacity | guidelines | pilot action | dissemination | communication
project_summary:
PROJECT TYPE: 3 - Governance Projects
~
MAIN INTERVENTION FIELD: 173. Enhancing institutional capacity of public authorities and stakeholders to implement territorial cooperation projects and initiatives in a cross-border, transnational, maritime and inter-regional context
~
SUMMARY: The overall objective of the JADEITE project is improve socio-environmental well-being in the Next Med programme area, through strengthened participatory governance processes. JADEITE works towards effective and impactful public policies and services supporting a just green transition.
Participatory governance refers to democratic mechanisms to involve stakeholders and citizens in public policy-making. In the Med area, such processes require testing, consolidation and integration into policy contexts. The concept is familiar, but processes are often unstructured and institutional capacity is often missing. Active engagement may not be a priority for people struggling with pressing socio-economic issues. Moreover, participatory processes must use opportunities that digital tools offer to reach previously unheard voices.
Public services to support the Green Transition represent an opportunity to test participatory governance process, addressing these challenges and helping to ensure that the transition is just and leaves nobody behind.
JADEITE involves partners from 5 countries, supported by a group of crucial associate partners, to map, test and consolidate participatory governance processes. The JADEITE Tool Kit will contain guidelines for offline methods and an online citizen-led, Challenge Mapping tool transferred from a previously funded Interreg Project. Territorial analysis and comprehensive research flank project activities.
Contributing to the New Agenda for the Mediterranean, SDG 17, Green Deal targets and various local policies, JADEITE will increase multi-level capacity to design, implement, monitor and build on participatory governance processes at territorial level, including steps forward towards a just green transition. Results will benefit public authorities, stakeholders but also individuals JADEITE territories. They will be integrated into local policy documents and international cooperation will be consolidated in a formal network.
~

project_name: Eco-nautical sea routes
project_summary:
As the world's largest tourist destination, Mediterranean ports are under intense tourist pressure. This makes their protected areas more vulnerable to overtourism.
The ECO-SEAROUTES project aims to create an eco-nautical route linking ports committed to: 1) respect a common sustainability standard in a transnational approach; 2) redistribute tourism flows to neighbouring coastal and inland areas.
To achieve this, the project encourages local decision-makers to create and develop new local tourist routes to less crowded areas and their cultural heritage. It will use a bottom-up and participatory approach, ready to be shared and replicated.

project_name: Supporting the implementation of blue and circular tourism in the Mediterranean
project_summary:
The RECIRCLE MED project for sustainable coastal tourism has two characteristics that make it stand out.
First, it integrates two approaches that are often considered separately: the circular economy, i.e. the reuse of resources, and the blue economy, i.e. the protection of coastal areas. How? By building on the tools and results of two previous successful projects: INCIRCLE and REBOOT MED.
Then, by bringing these approaches to smaller tourist destinations that do not have the capacity or funding to implement them. How? Through a step-by-step transfer process that facilitates their adoption and ensures the development of local plans tailored to their needs.

project_name: Transforming Rivers by Reducing Aquatic Plastic Pollution
project_summary:
TRAP addresses the critical transnational challenge of aquatic plastic pollution through innovative, eco-friendly solutions to reduce plastic litter reaching the Atlantic Ocean. The main goal is to produce guidelines to design hotspots to trap and retain plastics in rivers and estuaries. It offers an innovative approach to tackle aquatic plastic pollution by combining advanced digital tools, nature-based solutions and community engagement.
TRAP brings positive change in the efficiency of plastic removal strategies and better-informed management practices.
Key outputs include a digital management tool for identifying plastic retention hotspots, field-tested retention solutions, and practical guidelines for decision-makers. Beneficiaries range from environmental agencies to local authorities and riverine communities.
The project leverages transnational cooperation to ensure knowledge sharing, creating scalable, field-tested solutions that can be adapted across the Atlantic Area.

project_name: Consolidating an Atlantic Network of Sustainable AstroTourism Destinations
partner_name: Agência de Desenvolvimento Regional do Alentejo (Departamento de Coesão e Sustentabilidade Territorial )
project_summary:
CANSTAR is an innovative, integrated and sustainable AstroTourism project aware of environmental preservation and local economic growth across multiple European regions. The main transnational challenge to tackle is the sustainable development of AstroTourism, addressing light pollution, environmental conservation&rural development. The project’s overall objective is to promote sustainable tourism by enhancing star-studded sky resources while reducing light pollution.
Expected changes include improved local economies, preserved natural resources, and better training for tourism professionals. Main outputs include the development of infrastructure, training programs, awareness tools, and regional marketing strategies. Local communities, tourism professionals, and stakeholders will benefit directly. CANSTAR will use a collaborative approach, involving multiple regions, to ensure the sharing of best practices and resources, strengthening the long-term sustainability of AstroTourism through transnational cooperation.

project_name: Consolidating an Atlantic Network of Sustainable AstroTourism Destinations
partner_name: MANCOMUNIDAD DE MUNICIPIOS SOSTENIBLES DE CANTABRIA (Tecnical Direction)
project_summary:
CANSTAR is an innovative, integrated and sustainable AstroTourism project aware of environmental preservation and local economic growth across multiple European regions. The main transnational challenge to tackle is the sustainable development of AstroTourism, addressing light pollution, environmental conservation&rural development. The project’s overall objective is to promote sustainable tourism by enhancing star-studded sky resources while reducing light pollution.
Expected changes include improved local economies, preserved natural resources, and better training for tourism professionals. Main outputs include the development of infrastructure, training programs, awareness tools, and regional marketing strategies. Local communities, tourism professionals, and stakeholders will benefit directly. CANSTAR will use a collaborative approach, involving multiple regions, to ensure the sharing of best practices and resources, strengthening the long-term sustainability of AstroTourism through transnational cooperation.

project_name: Transnational R&D&I cooperation network to foster the competitiveness and sustainability of the Blue Biotech sector in the Atlantic Area territory
partner_name: UNIVERSIDADE DE VIGO (University of Vigo Marine Research Center)
project_summary:
Considering the characteristics of the Blue Biotech sector of the 8 AA regions involved, and their respective R&D&I strengths, our network MARINNONET focuses on 3 Marine Biotechnology Platforms:
BP1 – Innovations for an efficient, sustainable, and resilient aquaculture: Advancing our knowledge in the physiology, nutrition, genetics, and breeding of farmed organisms, as well as disease control methods. This contributes to improvements in fishing and shellfish farming activities, such as offering solutions for species identification and combating phenomena like red tides.
BP2 – Omic and observation technologies for preserving marine biodiversity and restore ocean health: Assisting to decipher the intricate biological and environmental interactions within marine ecosystems, including biodiversity and protection measures. Providing public administrations with the necessary tools and knowledge required to adapt to the effects of global climate change and safeguarding human health and well-being.
BP3 – Marine-derived products for industrial applications: Technologies for the isolation and processing of materials and bioactives from marine organisms, focusing on employing environmentally-friendly methods and approaches applied to marine by-products or under-exploited resources, towards their sustainability application in biomedicine, pharma, cosmetics and food.
specific_objective: RSO1.1. Developing and enhancing research and innovation capacities and the uptake of advanced technologies

project_name: Just and Green Transition through Participatory Governance
programme: 2021 - 2027 Interreg VI-B NEXT Mediterranean Sea Basin (NEXT MED)
topic_keywords: capacity building | capacity | guidelines | pilot action | dissemination | communication
project_summary:
PROJECT TYPE: 3 - Governance Projects
~
MAIN INTERVENTION FIELD: 173. Enhancing institutional capacity of public authorities and stakeholders to implement territorial cooperation projects and initiatives in a cross-border, transnational, maritime and inter-regional context
~
SUMMARY: The overall objective of the JADEITE project is improve socio-environmental well-being in the Next Med programme area, through strengthened participatory governance processes. JADEITE works towards effective and impactful public policies and services supporting a just green transition.
Participatory governance refers to democratic mechanisms to involve stakeholders and citizens in public policy-making. In the Med area, such processes require testing, consolidation and integration into policy contexts. The concept is familiar, but processes are often unstructured and institutional capacity is often missing. Active engagement may not be a priority for people struggling with pressing socio-economic issues. Moreover, participatory processes must use opportunities that digital tools offer to reach previously unheard voices.
Public services to support the Green Transition represent an opportunity to test participatory governance process, addressing these challenges and helping to ensure that the transition is just and leaves nobody behind.
JADEITE involves partners from 5 countries, supported by a group of crucial associate partners, to map, test and consolidate participatory governance processes. The JADEITE Tool Kit will contain guidelines for offline methods and an online citizen-led, Challenge Mapping tool transferred from a previously funded Interreg Project. Territorial analysis and comprehensive research flank project activities.
Contributing to the New Agenda for the Mediterranean, SDG 17, Green Deal targets and various local policies, JADEITE will increase multi-level capacity to design, implement, monitor and build on participatory governance processes at territorial level, including steps forward towards a just green transition. Results will benefit public authorities, stakeholders but also individuals JADEITE territories. They will be integrated into local policy documents and international cooperation will be consolidated in a formal network.
~

project_name: Eco-nautical sea routes
project_summary:
As the world's largest tourist destination, Mediterranean ports are under intense tourist pressure. This makes their protected areas more vulnerable to overtourism.
The ECO-SEAROUTES project aims to create an eco-nautical route linking ports committed to: 1) respect a common sustainability standard in a transnational approach; 2) redistribute tourism flows to neighbouring coastal and inland areas.
To achieve this, the project encourages local decision-makers to create and develop new local tourist routes to less crowded areas and their cultural heritage. It will use a bottom-up and participatory approach, ready to be shared and replicated.

project_name: Sustainable Microalgae and Cyanobacteria-Based Bioactive Compounds for Cosmeceuticals and Nutraceuticals
topic_keywords: knowledge transfer | pilot action | pilot actions | uptake | scalable | sme | smes
project_summary:
A pioneering initiative set to transform the cosmeceutical and nutraceutical industries by utilizing bioactives and biopolymers from microalgae. This project introduces innovative, low-impact methods to produce high-value ingredients, replacing synthetic additives while reducing water use and minimizing waste.
By optimizing microalgae cultivation and recovering valuable by-products, the project demonstrates scalable solutions that support a shift toward renewable, eco-friendly alternatives. Key outputs include pilot actions, innovative biotechnological solutions, and high-value bio-based products for industrial adoption, benefiting SMEs, researchers, and blue biotechnology industries.
With a transnational approach, the initiative integrates regional expertise, fosters knowledge transfer, and drives sustainable growth in the Atlantic’s blue economy. By accelerating innovation and competitiveness in the bioeconomy sector,it paves the way for a more sustainable future.

project_name: Climate Resilience and Adaptation in Aquaculture. Testing innovative technologies and ecosystem-based solutions for the resilience of Atlantic aquaculture to climate change.
partner_name: ASOCIACIÓN BIOGA – CLUSTER TECNOLÓXICO EMPRESARIAL DE CIENCIAS DA VIDA (Internationalisation)
project_summary:
The project strengthens the resilience of Atlantic aquaculture to climate change through ecosystem-based, technologically proven or in-development solutions (IMTA, FBNM, halophytes and others). It evaluates the vulnerability of aquaculture species and environments to changes in temperature, salinity and water quality, ensuring the sustainability of the sector, and promotes climate adaptation and risk prevention through innovative methodologies and restoration of key ecosystems, strengthening climate governance and participation.
The results include a data lake with climate projections, interactive vulnerability maps, adaptive management protocols and an early warning system for diseases. Transnational cooperation will facilitate the exchange of knowledge and the development of scalable solutions that boost the environmental and economic sustainability of the aquaculture sector.

project_name: High Power Test Sites to consolidate industrialisation of FOW in Europe
partner_name: CONSORCIO PARA EL DISENO, CONSTRUCCION, EQUIPAMIENTO Y EXPLOTACION DE LA PLATAFORMA OCEANICA DE CANARIAS (Innovation, Science and Technology)
project_summary:
The development of floating offshore wind technologies is accelerating significantly but different technical, environmental, and industrial challenges remain to be addressed. With less than 0.25GW installed worldwide in 2024 and the commissioning of the first commercial projects starting from 2030, the sector is still emerging. Currently, there is no test site in Europe capable of hosting floating wind turbines with a unit capacity greater than 10 MW.
HiPoTeSis aims to provide Europe with a coherent vision for testing high-power floating wind technologies, with the Atlantic region as the logical spearhead.
The objectives are to: - Create a consolidated European partnership in the Atlantic region focused on innovation in the floating wind sector; - Set the grounds for a transnational testing infrastructure with shared governance; - Provide common and operational responses to challenges faced by test sites to maximize future benefits for the territories involved.

project_name: Mediterranean Joint Initiative for Climate Adaptation and Risk Prevention
programme: 2021 - 2027 Interreg VI-B NEXT Mediterranean Sea Basin (NEXT MED)
topic_keywords: capacity building | capacity | capability | capabilities | technology transfer | dissemination | awareness | awareness raising | communication | platform
project_summary:
PROJECT TYPE: 1 - Thematic projects
~
MAIN INTERVENTION FIELD: 060. Adaptation to climate change measures and prevention and management of climate related risks: others, e.g. storms and drought (including awareness raising, civil protection and disaster management systems, infrastructures and ecosystem based approaches)
~
SUMMARY: The Mediterranean Sea is particularly sensitive to climate change, warming 20% faster than the global average while already under significant anthropogenic pressures like shipping, fishing, and tourism. Issues such as rising sea water temperatures, increasing salinity and invasive species are already negatively impacting marine ecosystems and affecting fisheries and tourism. The eastern Mediterranean in particular is a hotspot of rising sea water temperatures and at the same time a major gap in terms of marine observations. Countries in this region are in urgent need of increased climate resilience and adaptation capacity. It is expected that the frequency and intensity of extreme events will increase. Such events include storm surges, erosion, flash floods, marine heat waves (MHW) and Mediterranean hurricanes (“Medicanes”). Further, coastal areas are susceptible to maritime safety and marine pollution incidents because of a lack of operational capacity and situational awareness of the marine environment (despite the high volume of ship traffic and coastal economic activity).
To respond to scientific and societal challenges in the area, there is the need for a sustainable and cost-effective multiplatform observing system. We propose the combined use of low-maintenance, user-friendly multiplatform observing systems for a more comprehensive 3D characterization of the region at multiple scales: High-Frequency Radars (HFRs), Ferryboxes, coastal fixed point observatories and in-situ sensors. The need for setting up cost-effective observing systems in the area is further justified by the clear north-south and west-east imbalance of monitoring capabilities. Setting up a multi-level, multi-sector, and cross-border approach in adapting to climate change is an urgent need, and it expected to enhance the capability of eastern Mediterranean countries to jointly develop and and enhance innovation and actions for climate change adaptation, risk prevention & disaster resilience.
~

MAIN INTERVENTION FIELD: 060. Adaptation to climate change measures and prevention and management of climate related risks: others, e.g. storms and drought (including awareness raising, civil protection and disaster management systems, infrastructures and ecosystem based approaches)
~
SUMMARY: The Mediterranean Sea is particularly sensitive to climate change, warming 20% faster than the global average while already under significant anthropogenic pressures like shipping, fishing, and tourism. Issues such as rising sea water temperatures, increasing salinity and invasive species are already negatively impacting marine ecosystems and affecting fisheries and tourism. The eastern Mediterranean in particular is a hotspot of rising sea water temperatures and at the same time a major gap in terms of marine observations. Countries in this region are in urgent need of increased climate resilience and adaptation capacity. It is expected that the frequency and intensity of extreme events will increase. Such events include storm surges, erosion, flash floods, marine heat waves (MHW) and Mediterranean hurricanes (“Medicanes”). Further, coastal areas are susceptible to maritime safety and marine pollution incidents because of a lack of operational capacity and situational awareness of the marine environment (despite the high volume of ship traffic and coastal economic activity).
To respond to scientific and societal challenges in the area, there is the need for a sustainable and cost-effective multiplatform observing system. We propose the combined use of low-maintenance, user-friendly multiplatform observing systems for a more comprehensive 3D characterization of the region at multiple scales: High-Frequency Radars (HFRs), Ferryboxes, coastal fixed point observatories and in-situ sensors. The need for setting up cost-effective observing systems in the area is further justified by the clear north-south and west-east imbalance of monitoring capabilities. Setting up a multi-level, multi-sector, and cross-border approach in adapting to climate change is an urgent need, and it expected to enhance the capability of eastern Mediterranean countries to jointly develop and and enhance innovation and actions for climate change adaptation, risk prevention & disaster resilience.
~

project_name: Nature based solutions for climate resilient Mediterranean cities

topic_keywords: toolkit | pilot action | awareness | awareness raising | communication | uptake | scalable | platform | online platform | beneficiaries
project_summary:
PROJECT TYPE: 1 - Thematic projects
~
MAIN INTERVENTION FIELD: 060. Adaptation to climate change measures and prevention and management of climate related risks: others, e.g. storms and drought (including awareness raising, civil protection and disaster management systems, infrastructures and ecosystem based approaches)
~
SUMMARY: CHALLENGE ADDRESSED
Extreme weather events represent one of the primary climate risks in the Mediterranean, with urban areas, where the majority of the population resides, being exceptionally vulnerable due to a combination of densely-built spaces, impervious surfaces, and high population density. Furthermore, the growing intensity and frequency of these events (a direct result of climate change) makes conventional infrastructure (such as drainage systems), on which current climate resilience approaches are largely dependent, increasingly inadequate. To address this common challenge, nature-based solutions represent a scalable, cost-effective and environmentally sustainable approach with increasing popularity worldwide, yet with limited applications in the Mediterranean.

PROJECT IMPACT
NBS4MED aspires to transform urban climate adaptation strategies in the Mediterranean by advancing the adoption of nature-based solutions and their broad integration in urban climate resilience planning. To this end, the project will:
-Strengthen organisational capacities to design and implement fit-for-purpose nature-based solutions.
-Stimulate improvements in urban climate adaptation policies in project territories.
-Enhance transnational cooperation and exchange of knowledge.

KEY OUTPUTS
-A common decision making framework to guide the operationalisation of nature-based solutions across the Mediterranean.
-5 replicable nature-based solutions implemented and tested in project territories.
-Local strategies to accelerate the uptake of nature-based solutions in 5 target municipalities.
-A transnational network to enhance cooperation in urban climate adaptation and advance the deployment of nature-based solutions across the Mediterranean.

FINAL BENEFICIARIES
Urban populations in project territories will increase their climate-resilience and benefit from improved quality of life as a result of the increased deployment of nature-based solutions.
~

project_summary:
PROJECT TYPE: 3 - Governance Projects
~
MAIN INTERVENTION FIELD: 173. Enhancing institutional capacity of public authorities and stakeholders to implement territorial cooperation projects and initiatives in a cross-border, transnational, maritime and inter-regional context
~
SUMMARY: As underlined in NEXTMED synergies and complementarities document, "With a 2030 target, the EU Mission Restore our Ocean and Waters (Mission Ocean) aims to protect and restore the health of our ocean and waters through research and innovation, citizen engagement and blue investments. The Mission’s new approach will address the ocean and waters as one and play a key role in achieving climate neutrality and restoring nature".
In order for Mission Ocean to reach its goals, it is necessary it is deployed uniformly and widely to help local authorities in planning and rolling out the green transition. Doing so is a real challenge for Municipalities and it is even more difficult for small ones due to a problem of lack of critical mass.
Reaching the critical mass and having an appropriate transition agenda ARE THE TWO MOST URGENT NEEDS to be solved for Mediterranean Municipalities in view of the green transition and climate neutrality.
TO START ANSWERING THESE NEEDS, in June 2023, the Municipality of Genova and the EU Mission Ocean launched the Coalition of Mayors for Mission Ocean and their Shared Transition Plan outlining the communalities and possible synergies among the local development plan of each municipality member of the Coalition.
The aim of SHARE4MED is to FULLY ANSWER THESE NEED by consolidating the Coalition of Mayors for Mission Ocean, to expand it in EU and to extend it to non EU countries with specific focus on the WESTMED initiative.
SHARE4MED EXPECTED CHANGE with respect to the current situation will be a significantly greater awareness of Municipalities on Mission Ocean and WESTMED initiatives and on their solutions and empowerment tools to be used jointly, through the common SHARE4MED action plan, in achieving the climate neutrality in their territories.
MAIN OUTPUTS are the Coalition of Mayors and its Shared Agenda, Action Plan and Governance DASHBOARD. Final beneficiaries are Municipalities (TG1), local economic operators (TG3) and citizens (TG2)
~

PROJECT TYPE: 3 - Governance Projects
~
MAIN INTERVENTION FIELD: 173. Enhancing institutional capacity of public authorities and stakeholders to implement territorial cooperation projects and initiatives in a cross-border, transnational, maritime and inter-regional context
~
SUMMARY: The need to reinforce social serivices and social rights in the MED area is urgent and particularly relevant in rural/remote/peripheral areas, increasingly suffering from depopulation and aging, often lacking of basic services’ provision or where the access to them is most difficult, leaving the population more exposed against both systemic shocks and ordinary life-cycle risks (ILO, 2022).
CLOSER aims to promote joint actions in the Mediterranean for the promotion of participatory governance at local and regional level, by: a) the creation of territorial “proximity networks” in Italy, Spain, Greece, Palestine, Jordan and Lebanon engaging local social actors – alongside with the public authorities - as relevant stakeholders that often fill the gap in the existing public service’ provision; and b) their capacity building in becoming facilitators of participatory processes of local governance for the co-design and delivery of social services aimed at improving the wellbeing of the population.
The trained local networks will jointly develop a strategical model of participatory governance that will be piloted in each of the 6 participating regions, for the provision of co-design bottom-up social services with a higher level of respondence to real needs of the involved contexts, thanks to the participation of all concerned actors and a consequent closeness of the delivered services to the individuals to whom they are directed. Participatory governance facilitates cooperation between stakeholders, transparency of institutional processes, direct engagement of the local population with a consequent higher co-responsibility and ownership of the decision-processes and sense of belonging, more effective and inclusive services’ delivery and improved territorial cohesion. The final goal is to connect and build the capacity of these networks in jointly designing solutions to provide social services, planned and delivered through participatory models of governance.
~

project_summary:
PROJECT TYPE: 3 - Governance Projects
~
MAIN INTERVENTION FIELD: 173. Enhancing institutional capacity of public authorities and stakeholders to implement territorial cooperation projects and initiatives in a cross-border, transnational, maritime and inter-regional context
~
SUMMARY: The FOOD GaP project aims at providing a significant contribution for a better cooperation governance in the Mediterranean, thus grasping the extraordinary opportunity offered by this novel priority in the NEXT-MED programme. The project aim is to support local public authorities and stakeholders to adopt improved governance models for local food policies development in the Mediterranean.
Food policies' focus is key to address complex and intertwined challenges threatening cities in both shores of the programme area (such as urbanization, resources depletion, food insecurity, import dependency, as the international crisis and COVID-19 demonstrated, rural-urban disconnections) and a coordination is needed both with local stakeholders and cross borders to reduce the pressures on local communities wellbeing, welfare and social and political stability.
FOOD GaP objective will be achieved by: i. fostering local public authorities capacities to coordinate their efforts within their administrations and with the local actors (institutions, civil society, academia, and economic and social partners), ii. designing local participatory governance models, iii. piloting them by testing the improved services, embedded by the local food policies’ development, iv. establishing the Mediterranean Coalition for local food policies development.
Ciheam Bari will lead the project with 4 local public authorities (Tyre, LB, the metropolitan City Mersin, TR, the Metropolitan city of Rome, IT, Houmt Souk, TN), EPLO, EL, and 5 Associate partners, representing EU and international networks and organizations. All together will contribute advocate for a city food diplomacy and create new avenues for extended collaborations within the programme area, other cities, international networks and higher policy levels.
~

topic_keywords: training | learning | education | workshop | capacity | methodologies | guidelines | exploitation | sme
project_summary:
The Tourism Carrying Capacity Limit (TCCL) tool developed in the ALTERECO project addresses the risk of overtourism and its impact on the natural and cultural heritage of Mediterranean destinations.
The TO CARE MED project will extend the TCCL tool to other coastal, island, rural and protected natural tourist destinations, focusing on two aspects. First, the TCCL tool will be adapted to each destination. Then, it will be transferred using a 'cascade training model'. This will bring methodologies from the transnational level to local learning and implementation.
specific_objective: RSO2.7. Enhancing protection and preservation of nature, biodiversity and green infrastructure, including in urban areas, and reducing all forms of pollution
outputs:
Destination Working Groups sharing ideas and solutions via thematic transnational workshops (EN)

Transnational Training Programs to train trainers and transfer locally the knowledge about ecotourism sustainability and principle of CCL (EN)

Optimized TCCL Tool for Ecotourism(EN)

Joint MED Ecotourism Strategy(EN) || - Guidelines for the formation and operations of Destination Working Groups DWG

- Educational resources on ecotourism sustainability

- Baseline Assessment Report

- Guideline for TCCL tool application in ecotourism destination

- Exploitation Plan

- Ecotourism joint Strategy

- Integrated Action Plans

project_name: Strategic Harmonization and Resource Enhancement in the MEDiterranean: Advancing Water Management and Innovation through Non-Conventional Resources.
topic_keywords: capacity building | capacity | presentation | uptake | reuse | scalable | implementation plan | sme | communities
project_summary:
SHARE.MedWATER delivers scalable solutions to reuse rainwater, greywater, and treated wastewater in rural and island regions across seven Mediterranean countries.

It sets up community-level pilots where rainwater is harvested to irrigate crops, greywater from homes is reused for toilet flushing, and treated wastewater supports green spaces. At district level, two large-scale demos coordinate water reuse across agriculture, tourism, and urban services.

Inspired by energy-sharing models, SHARE.MedWATER builds a framework for managing and distributing non-conventional water sources collectively. A decision-support tool helps authorities weigh costs, environmental impact, and user needs. Through transnational cooperation, it aims to reduce dependence on freshwater, strengthen climate resilience, and support EU and regional water strategies.
specific_objective: RSO1.1. Developing and enhancing research and innovation capacities and the uptake of advanced technologies
outputs:
Transnational Cooperation for Innovative and Inclusive Water Reuse Strategies(EN)

Decentralized Water Management Solutions for Agriculture and Urban Communities(EN)

Cross-Border Framework Strengthening SHARE.MedWATER water community model fostering reuse and collaboration(EN) || - Regulatory synthesis report

- Pilot: Rainwater Treatment System for Agriculture in Western Macedonia (GR)

- Pilot: Community-Based Greywater Treatment and Reuse Model in Larnaca (CY)

- Pilot: Heritage-driven Rainwater Collection and Reuse in Gjirokastër, Albania (AL).

- Pilot: Rainwater Harvesting and Treatment for Climate Resilience in Cervia REC, Italy.

- Pilot: Rice field water reuse for protection of coastal areas in Delta del Ebro, Spain

- Pilot: Restoring the function of historic communal rainwater harvesting systems in Vittoriosa, Malta

- Pilot: Wastewater Reuse System Design and Implementation Plan for Lozzi, Corsica

- Video presentation

project_name: Promoting a Circular Economy with Low Plastic Zones on Mediterranean Islands
topic_keywords: learning | methodology | guidelines | replication | business support | platform
project_summary:
LooP Zone takes on one of the Mediterranean’s most visible challenges: the plastic waste crisis on tourist islands. With over 200 million visitors each year, and single-use plastics (SUPs) products, making up more than 60% of marine litter, island territories like Formentera, Salina, Skiathos, Vis, and Gozo face growing pressure—especially as most local waste management systems can’t keep up.

LooP Zone creates “low plastic zones” where municipalities, tourism businesses, and local innovators act together to prevent and phase out SUPs. Hotels, beach bars and restaurants receive tools to assess their plastic footprint and adopt sustainable and reusable alternatives; new value chains for SUP-free products are supported through innovation labs; and local policies are strengthened to align with national bans.

The model, built on a 3-pillar approach—policy, business support, and innovation—is tested locally, transferred and accessible via a digital platform, and scaled through a regional island network. The goal: transform the tourism sector from a take-make-waste model to a circular economy that works for both people and the sea.
specific_objective: RSO2.6. Promoting the transition to a circular and resource efficient economy

project_name: Development of Sustainable Astrotourism in Euro-MED Regions
partner_name: Centar za turistički razvoj i saradnju
project_summary:
Astrotourism is a new type of tourism in nature areas that focuses on the observation of celestial objects. Specifically, it consists of hiking and astronomy tours, observation of the nocturnal fauna, off-grid hotels, tourist services offered by astronomical observatories...

The MedSkyTour project has two main objectives: to develop this type of tourism and to implement actions aimed at reducing light pollution. In this way, the eight regions involved are not only creating new growth opportunities for sustainable tourism but are also generating real benefits for nocturnal biodiversity.

project_name: Inclusive Coastal Resilience in the Euro-Med
partner_name: Centar za regionalne aktivnosti Programa prioritetnih akcija
topic_keywords: course | workshop | methodology | methodologies | dissemination | communication | sme
project_summary:
INCORE-MED brings citizens back into the heart of coastal planning. In Italy, Spain, Portugal, and Croatia, the project tests two advanced participation tools to improve coastal and marine policies: SWAP and Democracy Labs.
The SWAP tool (Scenario Workshops + Adaptation Pathways) helps local actors co-design realistic climate adaptation plans by blending community visions with expert climate projections, using scenario mapping and multicriteria analysis. As for Democracy Labs, it creates discussion spaces where citizens and authorities co-develop new rules and formats for public engagement in marine planning.
These tools are deployed to: 1) create and update participatory cartographies and climate risk perception maps; 2) integrate nature-based solutions in local action plans; 3) disseminate the methodology to new territories through methodological insights and online courses.
specific_objective: RSO2.4. Promoting climate change adaptation and disaster risk prevention and resilience, taking into account eco-system based approaches

project_summary:
INCORE-MED brings citizens back into the heart of coastal planning. In Italy, Spain, Portugal, and Croatia, the project tests two advanced participation tools to improve coastal and marine policies: SWAP and Democracy Labs.
The SWAP tool (Scenario Workshops + Adaptation Pathways) helps local actors co-design realistic climate adaptation plans by blending community visions with expert climate projections, using scenario mapping and multicriteria analysis. As for Democracy Labs, it creates discussion spaces where citizens and authorities co-develop new rules and formats for public engagement in marine planning.
These tools are deployed to: 1) create and update participatory cartographies and climate risk perception maps; 2) integrate nature-based solutions in local action plans; 3) disseminate the methodology to new territories through methodological insights and online courses.

project_summary:
The Mediterranean tourism sector is largely made up of small and family businesses. That is why GreenSET develops a business support infrastructure to encourage as many of them as possible to innovate services they provide to reduce their environmental impact and better use resources.
Each business will create new competitive and sustainable service, enabling new revenues from existing resources and capabilities, supported by local MED experts, trained and certified in service innovation methodology. In this way, GreenSET aims for the long-term adoption of sustainable practices by a wide range of businesses, with each successful experience having the potential to spread to other ones!

project_name: Monitoring cumulative Impact to guide mitigation and RestorAtion in the MediterrAnean Region
partner_name: Μεσογειακό Γραφείο Πληροφόρησης για το Περιβάλλον τον Πολιτισμό και την Αειφόρο Ανάπτυξη
topic_keywords: training | training materials | capacity | methodology | toolkit | toolkits | stakeholder engagement | presenting | sme
project_summary: Humans are placing increasing strain on ecosystems exposing them to multiple, concurrent stressors. The Mediterranean region is a biodiversity hotspot hosting almost half of all the plant species, about a third of all the vertebrate species on our planet and an exceptional number of species found nowhere else in the world. This biodiversity, however, is heavily exposed to stressors like legacy and emerging contaminants, marine litter including microplastics, noise, habitat destruction from ever-increasing human activities, exacerbated by climate change, resulting in long-term impacts on ecosystem stability. These stressors are often tackled in isolation. The urgency to address these threats and the cumulative impact of multiple stressors requires cohesive, transnational efforts that combine fragmented knowledge to develop a new, integrated approach. In response to these challenges, the MIRAMAR project has identified three ecologically significant ecosystems in the Euro-Mediterranean region: seagrass meadows, wetlands, and habitats of endangered species. Seagrass meadows serve as crucial breeding, feeding, and sheltering grounds for numerous species, are vital for oxygen production and as blue carbon sinks. Wetlands are biodiverse transition ecosystems containing river deltas, brackish waters, salt lakes, and marshes, supporting a variety of flora and fauna, that play a critical role in maintaining ecological balance. Healthy endangered species habitats, including those of seabirds, cetaceans and wetland birds, are vital for the survival of these vulnerable populations. MIRAMAR’s 8 partners represent 6 countries across the Euro-Med region (Albania, Croatia, France, Greece, Italy, and Spain), and come from academia (UNISI), research institutes (CSIC, HCMR, SZN, RBI), NGO networks (MIO-ECSDE), as well as regional (OEC) and national authorities (MTE).Thanks to this transnational collaboration, MIRAMAR aims to develop an innovative harmonized methodology to monitor cumulative impact of selected stressors in 9 pilot areas in the three identified habitats. Drawing support and upscaling previous project results (INTERREG, ADRION, LIFE, H2020) MIRAMAR will design, test, and validate a new holistic monitoring method to identify suitable nature-based mitigation and restoration solutions. By aligning with national monitoring strategies and the management plans of parks and protected areas, MIRAMAR will address the common challenge of preventing, mitigating and restoring environmental degradation of these ecosystems. MIRAMAR is committed to aligning with key environmental initiatives and programs such as, Zero Pollution Action Plan of the European Green Deal, EU Biodiversity Strategy for 2030, EU Marine Strategy Framework Directive, Barcelona Convention's Integrated Monitoring and Assessment Programme, Union for the Mediterranean, UN Environment Programme/Mediterranean Action Plan, Ramsar Convention on Wetlands, among others. These frameworks emphasize the interconnected nature of climate change, biodiversity loss, land and sea degradation, stressing the importance of addressing these challenges in a coherent, synergetic, and holistic manner based on best available scientific knowledge.By engaging with these international agreements and frameworks, MIRAMAR will contribute to global efforts to tackle environmental issues and promote sustainable development practices. MIRAMAR’s collaborative approach and focus on stakeholder engagement, benefitting from Living Labs, underscores our commitment to harnessing collective expertise and fostering innovative solutions to address the complex challenges facing the Euro-Mediterranean region. Fourteen associate partners, representing key stakeholders in each targeted pilot area, will be proactively engaged in the project. They will see their scientific knowledge and capacity to implement environmental management practices and policies increase; more importantly, they will benefit from the project outcomes through the monitoring protocols and operational roadmap. This process of engagement will take concrete form with the signing of Declaration of Intent to adopt MIRAMARs harmonised monitoring protocol to assess cumulative impacts, as a key commitment to identify the "best" mitigation and restoration solutions. Through partnerships with diverse stakeholders, alignment with international initiatives, and a focus on science-based solutions, MIRAMAR is positioned to make a meaningful impact to protect nature, biodiversity, and green infrastructure for the benefit of current and future generations. With a comprehensive and inclusive approach, MIRAMAR aims to lead the way in addressing complex environmental challenges and charting a resilient, biodiverse, and sustainable future for all.

project_name: Fostering innovative and sustainable bio-waste management and circular bio-economy in the Mediterranean area
project_summary: CirBioWaste helps Mediterranean regions improve bio-waste management by promoting sustainable, circular practices. Despite EU rules, only 16% of food waste is properly collected and recycled, with many areas still relying on landfills. The project supports local governments and universities in six countries by adapting proven solutions, improving collection systems, boosting composting, and encouraging use of organic fertilizers. It combines local and international efforts, offering training, tools, and shared strategies to reduce environmental impact and support a circular bioeconomy across the EU MED region+B20.

project_name: Port-City Regions To Wave Med Cruise Destinations As Smart, Sustainable And Striking Eco-Tourism Systems
partner_name: Luka Koper, pristaniški in logistični sistem, delniška družba
topic_keywords: workshop | pilot action | pilot actions | platform | online platform
project_summary:
Cruise tourists are increasingly seeking tailor-made inland tours. They want to discover more authentic places in a sustainable way, in tours that suit their curiosity as well as their physical abilities.
The eco-tourism platform PortCREW will be tested in 5 Mediterranean cruise ports located in Spain, France, Italy, Slovenia, and Greece. What is it about? Providing cruise managers, port or tourism authorities with new inland tourism packages. How will it help tourism management? By avoiding overtourism in the same seaside areas, valorising new natural and cultural assets and increasing the social acceptance of the cruise sector in MED cities.

project_summary:
The escalating effects of climate change pose significant threats to cities; in particular, heavy rainfall, flooding, rising extreme temperatures, and urban heat island effects significantly impact the urban environment, life quality, and health in the Mediterranean. The Re-Act Schools project addresses these challenges by focusing on climate change adaptation and risk prevention in school outdoor spaces. Schoolyards, especially in cities where public green living areas are limited, are valuable assets that offer unique opportunities to test and promote Nature-based and innovative technological solutions for climate change mitigation and adaptation capable to reduce the climate hazard.

To achieve this goal, the project brings together a diverse and complementary partnership that comprises 2 research institutions, 5 public bodies, and a territorial development agency from 6 Mediterranean countries (Italy, Spain, France, Croatia, Bulgaria, and Cyprus), that will work together to transform 6 schoolyards through Pilot Actions into green, climate-resilient and innovative spaces that promote healthy and active lifestyles among students and the wider community, according to the goals of the New European Bauhaus and Green Deal Program.

The testing activity will be accompanied by a capacity-building phase finalised to define an innovative design methodology to mitigate heat stress, manage water resources efficiently, enhance biodiversity, and offer climate shelters to the citizens in the Mediterranean urban context based on the use of ENVI-met microclimatic simulations and Digital Urban Climate Twin models to test advanced methods for analysing, designing, and monitoring climate-resilient schoolyards.

The knowledge developed during the project will be collected in an innovative Action Plan and a multimedia tool to support the Public Administration of the Med Area in managing the regenerative transformation of schoolyards and public spaces.

project_name: COMMON MAC SPACE STRATEGY AS A REFERENCE FOR THE DEVELOPMENT OF TRANSREGIONAL RIS3 STRATEGIES
programme: 2014 - 2020 INTERREG V-A Spain - Portugal (Madeira - Açores - Canarias (MAC))
topic_keywords: training | pilot action | pilot actions | awareness | professionals
project_summary:
RIS3_NET2 is a project that proposes to work on three main lines:

1. Value the analysis and planning developed in the drafting of the Smart Specialisation Strategy of MAC Space through concrete actions.

2. Position the MAC Space as a reference in the elaboration of Smart Specialisation Strategies as it is the first to carry out this cooperation between three regions.

3. To bring the Macaronesian society and its professionals closer to the knowledge of the implications of an RIS3 and the existence and importance of the Smart Specialisation Strategy of MAC Space to achieve a greater alignment of the projects with the established development priorities, greater participation of these regions in cooperation projects, etc.
To this end, it will carry out an action plan, pilot actions for each priority and awareness-raising and training actions, which will in turn feed on other cross-cutting activities.

project_name: ChIMERA for Urban Sustainability
partner_name: Région autonome Friuli Venezia Giulia - Département de la culture et des sports
topic_keywords: capacity building | capacity | methodology | guidelines | sme | smes
project_summary: Cultural and creative industries (CCIs) represent highly innovative SMEs. For participating regions CCIs is a strategic sector and can play an important role in the transition towards the EU green deal and urban sustainability. Indeed, ChIMERA + aims at transferring the ChIMERA process and methodology for the growth of regional CCI subsectors promoting the sustainable urban development. In particular, the process focuses on regional CCI sector analysis, regional working groups involving main stakeholders and frontrunners analysis. The approach to transfer ChIMERA methodology is based on a mentoring and capacity building process, where giver partners adapt ChIMERA methodology to receivers’ needs and support receiver partners in implementing ChIMERA process till the development of strategic action plans for their specific contexts.Such actions are developed by local working groups composed by stakeholders, end-users and competent policy making levels of receiver territories: that approach ensures the ownership of the methodology and the adoption of strategic action plans by the receiver competent services. Last, at the end of transferring process giver partners outline guidelines for policy makers of MED area to support CCI sector and its role in achieving EU green deal objectives and urban sustainability.

project_name: Transforming Rivers by Reducing Aquatic Plastic Pollution
partner_name: Instituto Superior Técnico (Departamento de Engenharia Civil, Arquitetura e Georrecursos)
project_summary:
TRAP addresses the critical transnational challenge of aquatic plastic pollution through innovative, eco-friendly solutions to reduce plastic litter reaching the Atlantic Ocean. The main goal is to produce guidelines to design hotspots to trap and retain plastics in rivers and estuaries. It offers an innovative approach to tackle aquatic plastic pollution by combining advanced digital tools, nature-based solutions and community engagement.
TRAP brings positive change in the efficiency of plastic removal strategies and better-informed management practices.
Key outputs include a digital management tool for identifying plastic retention hotspots, field-tested retention solutions, and practical guidelines for decision-makers. Beneficiaries range from environmental agencies to local authorities and riverine communities.
The project leverages transnational cooperation to ensure knowledge sharing, creating scalable, field-tested solutions that can be adapted across the Atlantic Area.
specific_objective: RSO2.7. Enhancing protection and preservation of nature, biodiversity and green infrastructure, including in urban areas, and reducing all forms of pollution

project_name: Sustainable Management of visitor flows for Resilient Tourism in Coastal Areas
partner_name: Munster Technological University (Hospitality, Culinary Arts & Tourism)
project_summary:
The Atlantic Area coast, both highly attractive and sensitive, is home to a multitude of challenges regarding climate change, with tourism being a major sector requiring evolution to succeed in adaptation. Some coastal areas are currently experiencing levels of tourist numbers that question the capacity of natural areas to accommodate visitors, facing a number of challenges, in particular the need to preserve the natural heritage of the Atlantic coastline.
Through cooperation between Atlantic Area regions facing these challenges, the project aims to provide tools for better understanding and managing coastal tourist flows, and to rethink access to these areas from a sustainable perspective. Precisely, the project will develop a common replicable strategy to ensure that visitors are better managed and welcomed. This strategy will be supported by testing pilot actions based on visitor flow management, alternative mobility and visitor empowerment, to determine the best practices to adopt.
specific_objective: RSO4.6. Enhancing the role of culture and sustainable tourism in economic development, social inclusion and social innovation

project_name: AQUAtic systems’ evaluation for the Mitigation of wAter scarcity in mediterranean islaNds and coastal tourist destinations under severe pressure
project_summary:
Two facts. Agriculture accounts for 80% of water consumption in the Euro-MED region. The daily water consumption of a tourist is 2 to 4 times higher than that of a household.
AQUAMAN aims to reduce water scarcity by taking action on three levels: 1) Increasing the use of non-conventional water sources such as rainwater or wastewater; 2) Reducing water demand in the most consuming sectors; 3) Optimising local water distribution systems.
AQUAMAN will not solve the problem on its own. But it can engage many actors to do so with its knowledge of transferable successful EU projects. Its Water Scarcity Mitigation Living Labs based in 6 countries will promote them, along with training programmes.

project_name: Atlantic Tracking with Lightwave Acoustic Sensing
project_summary:
ATLAS pioneers the use of Distributed Acoustic Sensing (DAS) on submarine telecommunication cables to provide a revolutionary, cost-effective solution for ocean monitoring, biodiversity conservation and coastal risk prevention. The project addresses major transnational challenges, including geohazard detection, marine noise pollution, and whale-ship collision prevention across the Atlantic, delivering critical insights for public authorities, maritime operators, and researchers.
The project’s outputs are ranging from seismic event warnings to marine biodiversity assessments, and enhance policy decisions, protect ocean ecosystems, and improve maritime safety. By leveraging international cooperation and deploying DAS-based methodologies at multiple sites along the Atlantic rim, ATLAS will set a new standard for sustainable ocean observation, aligning with European environmental directives and ensuring a long-term impact on marine conservation and risk prevention.

project_name: Action for Communities in Teleassistance and Integration of Vulnerable populations facing Natural hazards
partner_name: European Regions Network for the Application of Communications Technology (N/A)
project_summary:
Social inclusion, and environmental and climate risks are two concepts that are usually seen as separate. However, we must understand the need to establish clear links between the fight against climate change and the defence of the most vulnerable people. Without this innovative vision, we will not be able to create a truly socially and environmentally just and resilient Atlantic Area.
Recent climate events force us to move towards environmental planning that takes into account the most vulnerable people, both for their protection and their empowerment. In this project, it pursues this dual purpose. On the one hand, it creates innovative mechanisms to support vulnerable people in case of extreme environmental risks such as floods, fires or droughts. Nonetheless, it creates methodologies to make this group a key actor in the fight against climate change and its consequences, demonstrating that pilots of maximum environmental effectiveness can be carried out through social inclusion.
suggested_angle: Structure project methods, outputs and pilot lessons into reusable training for target beneficiaries.

project_name: Optimizing the Algal Biomass Value Chains for Sustainable Economic Growth in the Atlantic Area
partner_name: ASOCIACIÓN BIOGA – CLUSTER TECNOLÓXICO EMPRESARIAL DE CIENCIAS DA VIDA (Internationalisation)
project_summary:
The project focuses on achieving an efficient and sustainable utilization of micro and macroalgae in the Atlantic Area by organizing and optimizing the entire algal biomass value chains.
It involves stakeholders mapping and cooperation, challenges analysis, models development powered with intelligent digital tools, and the generation of market-oriented prototypes for the cosmetics and food sectors, while fostering collaboration across Spain, Portugal, France and Ireland.
The approach of the project integrates sustainable practices, innovative technologies, and stakeholder insights to optimize harvesting, valorisation, production and consumer involvement. By aligning industry needs with environmental and market demands, the project promotes a sustainable and economic growth in the algae-based biomass industry.

project_name: Atlantic Tracking with Lightwave Acoustic Sensing
partner_name: Instituto de Engenharia de Sistemas e Computadores, Tecnologia e Ciência (Centro de Robótica e Sistemas Autónomos)
project_summary:
ATLAS pioneers the use of Distributed Acoustic Sensing (DAS) on submarine telecommunication cables to provide a revolutionary, cost-effective solution for ocean monitoring, biodiversity conservation and coastal risk prevention. The project addresses major transnational challenges, including geohazard detection, marine noise pollution, and whale-ship collision prevention across the Atlantic, delivering critical insights for public authorities, maritime operators, and researchers.
The project’s outputs are ranging from seismic event warnings to marine biodiversity assessments, and enhance policy decisions, protect ocean ecosystems, and improve maritime safety. By leveraging international cooperation and deploying DAS-based methodologies at multiple sites along the Atlantic rim, ATLAS will set a new standard for sustainable ocean observation, aligning with European environmental directives and ensuring a long-term impact on marine conservation and risk prevention.

project_name: Atlantic Wave Energy Sustainable Deployment Initiative
partner_name: UNIVERSIDADE DE VIGO (University of Vigo Marine Research Center)
project_summary:
AWESDI is a transnational initiative introducing an innovative approach to accelerate the deployment of wave energy systems in the Atlantic Area. Coastal regions in Portugal, Spain, France and Ireland share vast wave energy potential but face common WE deployment barriers, including fragmented regulatory frameworks, infrastructure limitations, and public acceptance challenges.
Unlike previous initiatives, AWESDI will adopt a systemic design approach to ensure that wave energy solutions are not only technically feasible but also commercially viable, socially inclusive and environmentally friendly, according to the unique characteristics of the most promising Atlantic sites. The project will deliver advanced sustainable design and holistic assessment tools, wave energy technology-/site-specific exploitation strategies, comprehensive and coordinated policy recommendations, and a joint Wave Energy Deployment Roadmap to accelerate the buildup of a cohesive and scalable market across the Atlantic regions.
specific_objective: RSO2.1. Promoting energy efficiency and reducing greenhouse gas emissions

project_name: Sustainable Atlantic Communities
project_summary: SAtComm aims to enable citizens from residential, SME, and industrial entities to mobilise as groups into Energy Communities and equip them with the digitalisation and legal tools to empower them to play a central role in the Clean Energy Transition. The project has 5 pilots locations in the 4 Member-States jurisdictions: an office building in the Irish city of Dingle, a community building in the French West Central Brittany area, the Port of Lamego in Portugal’s Douro sub-region, a mixture of industrial and residential premises in the Spanish Coruña region, and the University of La Laguna in Canarias, Spain, surrounding student accommodation premises. The objective is to enable local communities to integrate renewable energy and smart grid concepts to improve their competitiveness, maximise their sustainability, and become “prosumers.”

project_name: AQUAtic systems’ evaluation for the Mitigation of wAter scarcity in mediterranean islaNds and coastal tourist destinations under severe pressure
project_summary:
Two facts. Agriculture accounts for 80% of water consumption in the Euro-MED region. The daily water consumption of a tourist is 2 to 4 times higher than that of a household.
AQUAMAN aims to reduce water scarcity by taking action on three levels: 1) Increasing the use of non-conventional water sources such as rainwater or wastewater; 2) Reducing water demand in the most consuming sectors; 3) Optimising local water distribution systems.
AQUAMAN will not solve the problem on its own. But it can engage many actors to do so with its knowledge of transferable successful EU projects. Its Water Scarcity Mitigation Living Labs based in 6 countries will promote them, along with training programmes.
specific_objective: RSO2.4. Promoting climate change adaptation and disaster risk prevention and resilience, taking into account eco-system based approaches

project_name: GRASPINNO PLUS
topic_keywords: training | capacity | methodology | exploitation | enterprise | enterprises | platform | communities
project_summary: GRASPINNOPLUS aims to transfer key deliverables/outputs developed & tested at transnational level within GRASPINNO project such as Data-Base, eGPP Support tool & Life Cycle Costing tool (Unified Platform Package) to new MED territories. These instruments are expected to generate territorial dynamics impacting on local and regional communities, in terms of decision-making procedures and policies.The new dynamics will also be favorably impacting on eco-innovation of clusters/networks for their products/services provision, focusing on GPP methods and other possible intervention areas such as Energy-Efficiency & other investment sectors. These impacts are key for the implementation of a sustainable, circular and aware public procurement considering all the costs that will be incurred during the lifetime of a product, public work or service. The transferring process will combine thematic training sessions & guided practical applications, which will lead the 4 receiver partners (regions & Chamber of enterprises)in designing concrete joint solutions adapted to specific green specs/criteria, eGPP support & LCC application plans in tenders with green products/services thus increasing their capacity in green growth.GRASPINNOPLUS will achieve the formal commitment of receivers to replicate the transferred methodology in their territories,taking into account specific local needs & challenges emerged during and after its completion.Exploitation of results will be also guaranteed by APs.

project_name: Action for Communities in Teleassistance and Integration of Vulnerable populations facing Natural hazards
project_summary:
Social inclusion, and environmental and climate risks are two concepts that are usually seen as separate. However, we must understand the need to establish clear links between the fight against climate change and the defence of the most vulnerable people. Without this innovative vision, we will not be able to create a truly socially and environmentally just and resilient Atlantic Area.
Recent climate events force us to move towards environmental planning that takes into account the most vulnerable people, both for their protection and their empowerment. In this project, it pursues this dual purpose. On the one hand, it creates innovative mechanisms to support vulnerable people in case of extreme environmental risks such as floods, fires or droughts. Nonetheless, it creates methodologies to make this group a key actor in the fight against climate change and its consequences, demonstrating that pilots of maximum environmental effectiveness can be carried out through social inclusion.
suggested_angle: Structure project methods, outputs and pilot lessons into reusable training for target beneficiaries.
next_action: Review partner website and project page; validate live outputs and training assets.

Nonetheless, it creates methodologies to make this group a key actor in the fight against climate change and its consequences, demonstrating that pilots of maximum environmental effectiveness can be carried out through social inclusion.
suggested_angle: Structure project methods, outputs and pilot lessons into reusable training for target beneficiaries.

project_name: Integrating Seasonal Thermal Energy Storage in the Mediterranean Region
programme: 2021 - 2027 Interreg VI-B NEXT Mediterranean Sea Basin (NEXT MED)
topic_keywords: toolkit | pilot action | pilot actions | dissemination | communication | uptake | platform
project_summary:
PROJECT TYPE: 1 - Thematic projects
~
MAIN INTERVENTION FIELD: 041. Energy efficiency renovation of existing housing stock, demonstration projects and supporting measures
~
SUMMARY: The Mediterranean faces common challenges related to energy consumption, climate change, and the need for sustainable solutions. Mediterranean countries spend more than 50% of their energy consumption on space heating and/or cooling. The Mediterranean climate is ideal for harnessing and storing solar thermal energy to reduce the energy demand of buildings toward a net zero energy building target.

STORM capitalizes on the Mediterranean's solar potential by integrating seasonal thermal energy storage (STES) in the building stock. STES is a mature technology that involves harnessing thermal energy during the sun-rich months and storing the energy in soil/rock/water via borehole thermal energy storage or tank thermal energy storage. The stored energy is utilized to heat spaces and provide domestic hot water or to cool spaces by powering innovative thermally-activated adsorption chillers with low grade heat. Despite it being a proven technology, there remains minimal deployment of STES in Mediterranean countries. This is attributed to the lack of regulations, data, and pilot demonstrations that prove the efficiency and feasibility of this technology in the Mediterranean.

Through 4 pilots that are jointly implemented in Beirut, Ajloun, and Palermo or showcased in Barcelona, STORM will generate localized data on efficiency gains and develop simulation models to speed up the uptake of this technology in decentralized applications in the Mediterranean. The project’s transnational implementation will lead to social, economic, and environmental benefits for owners/occupants of residential, commercial, and public buildings and energy grid operators, regulatory bodies, and policy makers who will benefit from an improved resilience and efficiency in the energy infrastructure. STORM aims to unlock the potential of seasonal thermal storage in the Mediterranean and advocate for a shift from conventional energy practices to a sustainable, energy-efficiency decentralized model.
~

project_name: CROSS-BORDER CHAMBERS' NEW SERVICES as driver FOR SUSTAINABLE FAIR TRADE OF TEXTILE INDUSTRY ACROSS MEDITERRANEAN
programme: 2021 - 2027 Interreg VI-B NEXT Mediterranean Sea Basin (NEXT MED)
project_summary:
PROJECT TYPE: 3 - Governance Projects
~
MAIN INTERVENTION FIELD: 173. Enhancing institutional capacity of public authorities and stakeholders to implement territorial cooperation projects and initiatives in a cross-border, transnational, maritime and inter-regional context
~
SUMMARY: The MEDinCHAMBERS project aims to enhance the cross-border governance model in the textile value chains in Italy, Spain, Tunisia, Egypt, and Turkey. The partner institutions have different organizations, diverse and not always shared information, independent operating procedures, and non-integrated tools. Therefore, it is necessary to take governance actions to structure a network of knowledge and establish lasting relationships, so that collaboration and support are effectively possible and mutual cooperation between the EU and MPC chambers of commerce, as already provided for by agreements in force at national and regional levels.
The goal is to build a shared common knowledge framework (hubs&detector) of the rules, risks, focusing on those linked to textile sector needs and constraints, resources, and to create a cross-border operational team (MEDinCHAMBERS) composed of several bilateral chambers of commerce in the Med area. It is important to note that the textile sector is export-oriented, with the European Union being the main destination market. Therefore, compliance with the new EU Strategy for Sustainable and Circular Textiles and recognizing the importance of the textiles sector, along with the urgent need for new sustainable and environmental approaches, the main goal is to promote a more circular, energy-saving, and sustainable textile sector in Italy, Spain, Egypt, Tunisia, and Turkey, by providing tailored support services and creating a cross-border alliance.
~

project_name: Just and Green Transition through Participatory Governance
programme: 2021 - 2027 Interreg VI-B NEXT Mediterranean Sea Basin (NEXT MED)
topic_keywords: capacity building | capacity | guidelines | pilot action | dissemination | communication
project_summary:
PROJECT TYPE: 3 - Governance Projects
~
MAIN INTERVENTION FIELD: 173. Enhancing institutional capacity of public authorities and stakeholders to implement territorial cooperation projects and initiatives in a cross-border, transnational, maritime and inter-regional context
~
SUMMARY: The overall objective of the JADEITE project is improve socio-environmental well-being in the Next Med programme area, through strengthened participatory governance processes. JADEITE works towards effective and impactful public policies and services supporting a just green transition.
Participatory governance refers to democratic mechanisms to involve stakeholders and citizens in public policy-making. In the Med area, such processes require testing, consolidation and integration into policy contexts. The concept is familiar, but processes are often unstructured and institutional capacity is often missing. Active engagement may not be a priority for people struggling with pressing socio-economic issues. Moreover, participatory processes must use opportunities that digital tools offer to reach previously unheard voices.
Public services to support the Green Transition represent an opportunity to test participatory governance process, addressing these challenges and helping to ensure that the transition is just and leaves nobody behind.
JADEITE involves partners from 5 countries, supported by a group of crucial associate partners, to map, test and consolidate participatory governance processes. The JADEITE Tool Kit will contain guidelines for offline methods and an online citizen-led, Challenge Mapping tool transferred from a previously funded Interreg Project. Territorial analysis and comprehensive research flank project activities.
Contributing to the New Agenda for the Mediterranean, SDG 17, Green Deal targets and various local policies, JADEITE will increase multi-level capacity to design, implement, monitor and build on participatory governance processes at territorial level, including steps forward towards a just green transition. Results will benefit public authorities, stakeholders but also individuals JADEITE territories. They will be integrated into local policy documents and international cooperation will be consolidated in a formal network.
~

project_name: Just and Green Transition through Participatory Governance
programme: 2021 - 2027 Interreg VI-B NEXT Mediterranean Sea Basin (NEXT MED)
topic_keywords: capacity building | capacity | guidelines | pilot action | dissemination | communication
project_summary:
PROJECT TYPE: 3 - Governance Projects
~
MAIN INTERVENTION FIELD: 173. Enhancing institutional capacity of public authorities and stakeholders to implement territorial cooperation projects and initiatives in a cross-border, transnational, maritime and inter-regional context
~
SUMMARY: The overall objective of the JADEITE project is improve socio-environmental well-being in the Next Med programme area, through strengthened participatory governance processes. JADEITE works towards effective and impactful public policies and services supporting a just green transition.
Participatory governance refers to democratic mechanisms to involve stakeholders and citizens in public policy-making. In the Med area, such processes require testing, consolidation and integration into policy contexts. The concept is familiar, but processes are often unstructured and institutional capacity is often missing. Active engagement may not be a priority for people struggling with pressing socio-economic issues. Moreover, participatory processes must use opportunities that digital tools offer to reach previously unheard voices.
Public services to support the Green Transition represent an opportunity to test participatory governance process, addressing these challenges and helping to ensure that the transition is just and leaves nobody behind.
JADEITE involves partners from 5 countries, supported by a group of crucial associate partners, to map, test and consolidate participatory governance processes. The JADEITE Tool Kit will contain guidelines for offline methods and an online citizen-led, Challenge Mapping tool transferred from a previously funded Interreg Project. Territorial analysis and comprehensive research flank project activities.
Contributing to the New Agenda for the Mediterranean, SDG 17, Green Deal targets and various local policies, JADEITE will increase multi-level capacity to design, implement, monitor and build on participatory governance processes at territorial level, including steps forward towards a just green transition. Results will benefit public authorities, stakeholders but also individuals JADEITE territories. They will be integrated into local policy documents and international cooperation will be consolidated in a formal network.
~

project_name: Eco-nautical sea routes
partner_name: Akcionarsko društvo za uslužne djelatnosti u međunarodnom pomorskom saobraćaju Luka Kotor A.D. Kotor
project_summary:
As the world's largest tourist destination, Mediterranean ports are under intense tourist pressure. This makes their protected areas more vulnerable to overtourism.
The ECO-SEAROUTES project aims to create an eco-nautical route linking ports committed to: 1) respect a common sustainability standard in a transnational approach; 2) redistribute tourism flows to neighbouring coastal and inland areas.
To achieve this, the project encourages local decision-makers to create and develop new local tourist routes to less crowded areas and their cultural heritage. It will use a bottom-up and participatory approach, ready to be shared and replicated.

project_name: Supporting the implementation of blue and circular tourism in the Mediterranean
partner_name: LIVING PROSPECTS ΥΠΗΡΕΣΙΕΣ ΑΝΑΠΤΥΞΗΣ ΚΑΙ ΠΕΡΙΒΑΛΛΟΝΤΟΣ ΕΠΕ
project_summary:
The RECIRCLE MED project for sustainable coastal tourism has two characteristics that make it stand out.
First, it integrates two approaches that are often considered separately: the circular economy, i.e. the reuse of resources, and the blue economy, i.e. the protection of coastal areas. How? By building on the tools and results of two previous successful projects: INCIRCLE and REBOOT MED.
Then, by bringing these approaches to smaller tourist destinations that do not have the capacity or funding to implement them. How? Through a step-by-step transfer process that facilitates their adoption and ensures the development of local plans tailored to their needs.

project_name: GRASPINNO PLUS
topic_keywords: training | capacity | methodology | exploitation | enterprise | enterprises | platform | communities
project_summary: GRASPINNOPLUS aims to transfer key deliverables/outputs developed & tested at transnational level within GRASPINNO project such as Data-Base, eGPP Support tool & Life Cycle Costing tool (Unified Platform Package) to new MED territories. These instruments are expected to generate territorial dynamics impacting on local and regional communities, in terms of decision-making procedures and policies.The new dynamics will also be favorably impacting on eco-innovation of clusters/networks for their products/services provision, focusing on GPP methods and other possible intervention areas such as Energy-Efficiency & other investment sectors. These impacts are key for the implementation of a sustainable, circular and aware public procurement considering all the costs that will be incurred during the lifetime of a product, public work or service. The transferring process will combine thematic training sessions & guided practical applications, which will lead the 4 receiver partners (regions & Chamber of enterprises)in designing concrete joint solutions adapted to specific green specs/criteria, eGPP support & LCC application plans in tenders with green products/services thus increasing their capacity in green growth.GRASPINNOPLUS will achieve the formal commitment of receivers to replicate the transferred methodology in their territories,taking into account specific local needs & challenges emerged during and after its completion.Exploitation of results will be also guaranteed by APs.

project_name: Routes of the Great Atlantic Explorations
project_summary:
Setting up the Atlantic network for the valorization of the great nautical expeditions is the overall project objective. This is totally innovative to date. This objective represents the transnational challenge jointly tackled by the partnership.
Added value brought by the figure of the Council of Europe Cultural Route will be applied to the valorization of the Great Atlantic Expeditions legacy. Transnational strategies and action plans will drive 6 project activities embedded in 3 work packages. Activities address the set-up of a scientific committee, of an Atlantic Association governance body, of a new Council of Europe Cultural Route, of the implementation of 4 Pilot actions and of enhancing visibility and innovation to the new Cultural Route. All of this could only be achievable on the basis of transnational cooperation of the project partnership and other territorial partners not involved in the project. The durability of the project outputs is ensured.
