BODAH aims at improving cooperation, joint planning and at better managing conflicting interests to improve the management of two kind of cultural destinations that can benefit and be affected by tourism and the pressure of local citizens: Old-historical city centres and Sites of particular cultural or heritage interest.
The project plans to take advantage of technology and innovation in management tools (Smart Cities), in particular, through the collection of existing - and generation of complementary - data (Big Data, Open Data, Internet of the Things, data provided through existing sensors, local systems or cameras, etc.). One of the key objectives is to develop new solutions to address social and public needs, contributing to create new alternative offers adapted to the interests of visitors to better distribute their crowd flows, as well as redistribute impacts (both, environmental and economic ones) on heritage sites, cities and monuments.
suggested_angle: Structure project methods, outputs and pilot lessons into reusable training for target beneficiaries.

project_name: Maintenance drones and robots to enhance renewable energy systems in the Atlantic Area
project_summary:
Aerospace and robotic technologies together with composites and additive manufacturing will be interweaved to produce full innovative solutions for a more efficient energy production. The use of support robots on solar panels and windmill engines will boost these clean energies in the area.
DURABLE will accelerate the deployment of renewable energies in the Atlantic Area through the validation and demonstration of previous developed aerospace technologies applied in robotics for operation and maintenance activities in wind and solar energy systems.
The supporting robots will be adapted to perform tasks of inspection/surveillance and reparation reducing the high costs of these activities favouring regional renewable energy production conditions.
The project will create an innovative institutional and technical cooperation network that will reduce the operation & maintenance costs in the renewable energy production, merging key technological and institutional actors from these critical sectors.

project_name: LABORATORIES TO MEASURE THE ENERGY EFFICIENCY OF ARTIFICIAL NIGHT LIGHT IN PROTECTED NATURAL AREAS OF MACARONESIA.
programme: 2014 - 2020 INTERREG V-A Spain - Portugal (Madeira - Açores - Canarias (MAC))
project_summary:
The EELabs project seeks to respond to the impact and threat that the loss of darkness due to the increasing use of Artificial Night Light (LAN) has in natural ecosystems, such as 30 % of vertebrates and 60 % of invertebrates -nocturnes and very sensitive to light-.
EELabs’ goal is to maximise the energy efficiency of new lighting technology — mainly LED — while minimising their effect in the form of light pollution — sky glare — on Macaronesia’s natural ecosystems. EELabs will propose activities to acquire a deep knowledge of both LED lighting and macaronesic natural ecosystems to achieve sustainable growth where the increase of LAN, as a consequence of economic development, does not compromise natural spaces by an excessive increase in light pollution.
suggested_angle: Structure project methods, outputs and pilot lessons into reusable training for target beneficiaries.
next_action: Review partner website and project page; validate live outputs and training assets.

project_name: EVOLUTIONARY MANAGEMENT OF THE ENDEMIC TERRESTRIAL PLANT DIVERSITY OF MACARONESIA THROUGH ACTIONS ON THREATENED SPECIES AND THE CREATION OF TOOLS OF GEOGRAPHICAL BIOINFORMATICS WITH APPLICATION TO THE CONSERVATION OF SPECIES, SPACES AND GENES.
programme: 2014 - 2020 INTERREG V-A Spain - Portugal (Madeira - Açores - Canarias (MAC))
project_summary: The NEXTGENDEM project complements genetic, taxonomic, ecological, geographical and computational lines of work to help managers make appropriate decisions based on the most current proven and validated scientific data at all times. The calculations will be made using various resources, from supercomputers (seeking the support of the Spanish Supercomputing Network, RES) to the bioinformatics computing services of the European Institute of Bioinformatics, belonging to the European Laboratory of Molecular Biology, through local computing resources based on graphics cards. As a result of the enrichment of the sample and data banks indispensable to achieve these objectives, the conservation status of 60 endangered endemisms in the Canary Islands and Cape Verde will be improved through various actions in the field, and the transnational cooperation network on biodiversity will be strengthened.
